sentences.zh: 曹沫者，魯人也，以勇力事魯莊公。

translations.literal: Cao Mo was a man of Lu who served Duke Zhuang of Lu through bravery and strength.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Mo was a native of Lu who served Duke Zhuang of Lu through his courage and martial prowess.

sentences.zh: 莊公好力。

translations.literal: Duke Zhuang valued strength.
translations.idiomatic: The duke admired physical strength.

sentences.zh: 曹沫為魯將，與齊戰，三敗北。

translations.literal: Cao Mo served as general of Lu, fought against Qi, and was defeated three times.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Mo served as a general for Lu, but suffered three defeats in battle against Qi.

sentences.zh: 魯莊公懼，乃獻遂邑之地以和。

translations.literal: Duke Zhuang of Lu was afraid, so he offered the territory of Sui to sue for peace.
translations.idiomatic: Alarmed, Duke Zhuang of Lu ceded the territory of Sui to make peace.

sentences.zh: 猶復以為將。

translations.literal: Yet he still kept him as general.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, the duke retained Cao Mo as his general.

translations.literal: Cao Mo was a man of Lu, served Lu Zhuang Gong with bravery and strength. Zhuang Gong delighted in strength. Cao Mo as Lu general, battled Qi, three times defeated retreated. Lu Zhuang Gong feared, thus offered Sui yi land to make peace. Still again made him general.

sentences.zh: 齊桓公許與魯會于柯而盟。

translations.literal: Duke Huan of Qi agreed to meet with Lu at Ke to form an alliance.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan of Qi consented to meet the ruler of Lu at Ke to conclude a treaty.

sentences.zh: 桓公與莊公既盟於壇上，曹沫執匕首劫齊桓公，桓公左右莫敢動，而問曰：「子將何欲？」

translations.literal: After Duke Huan and Duke Zhuang had concluded their covenant upon the altar, Cao Mo seized a dagger and took Duke Huan of Qi hostage. None of the duke's attendants dared move, and the duke asked: 'What is it you want?'
translations.idiomatic: After the two dukes had sealed their covenant atop the altar, Cao Mo drew a dagger and seized Duke Huan of Qi. Not one of the duke's attendants dared stir, and the duke demanded, "What is it you want?"

sentences.zh: 曹沫曰：「齊彊魯弱，而大國侵魯亦甚矣。

translations.literal: Cao Mo said: 'Qi is strong and Lu is weak, and the great state's encroachment upon Lu has been extreme.'
translations.idiomatic: Cao Mo replied, "Qi is powerful and Lu is weak, yet your great state has encroached upon Lu beyond all measure."

sentences.zh: 今魯城壞即壓齊境，君其圖之。」

translations.literal: Now the walls of Lu when destroyed press upon Qi's borders; my lord, consider this.
translations.idiomatic: Now the crumbling walls of Lu press against the very borders of Qi. My lord, I urge you to reconsider."

sentences.zh: 桓公乃許盡歸魯之侵地。

translations.literal: Duke Huan then agreed to return all the lands seized from Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan thereupon agreed to restore every last territory taken from Lu.

sentences.zh: 既已言，曹沫投其匕首，下壇，北面就群臣之位，顏色不變，辭令如故。

translations.literal: When the words had been spoken, Cao Mo cast away his dagger, descended the altar, faced north, and took his place among the ministers; his expression did not change and his speech was as before.
translations.idiomatic: His terms secured, Cao Mo cast aside the dagger, descended from the altar, and took his place facing north among the assembled ministers. His expression remained unchanged, his speech as composed as before.

sentences.zh: 桓公怒，欲倍其約。

translations.literal: Duke Huan was furious and wished to break the agreement.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan was furious and wanted to renege on the agreement.

sentences.zh: 管仲曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Guan Zhong said: 'You must not.'
translations.idiomatic: Guan Zhong counseled, "You must not."

sentences.zh: 夫貪小利以自快，棄信於諸侯，失天下之援，不如與之。」

translations.literal: To covet a small advantage for momentary satisfaction, abandon good faith with the feudal lords, and lose the support of all under Heaven — better to give it to him.
translations.idiomatic: To grasp at a petty gain for a moment's satisfaction, only to forfeit the trust of the feudal lords and lose the support of all under Heaven — better to give him what he asks."

sentences.zh: 於是桓公乃遂割魯侵地，曹沫三戰所亡地盡復予魯。

translations.literal: Thereupon Duke Huan returned the lands taken from Lu, and all the territory lost by Cao Mo in three battles was fully restored to Lu.
translations.idiomatic: And so Duke Huan returned the seized territories, and all the lands that Cao Mo had lost in three defeats were fully restored to Lu.

translations.literal: Qi Huan Gong permitted meet with Lu at Ke to ally. Huan Gong with Zhuang Gong having allied on altar, Cao Mo grasped dagger hostage Qi Huan Gong. Huan Gong attendants none dared move, asked: 'You will what desire?' Cao Mo said: 'Qi strong Lu weak, great state invade Lu also extreme. Now Lu city broken then presses Qi border, lord consider it.' Huan Gong thus permitted completely return Lu invaded land. Already spoken, Cao Mo threw his dagger, descended altar, north faced took ministers' position, complexion unchanged, words as before. Huan Gong angered, wished break agreement. Guan Zhong said: 'Cannot. Greed small benefit self-pleasure, abandon trust among feudal lords, lose world support, better give him.' Thus Huan Gong then cut Qi invaded land, Cao Mo three battles lost land completely restored to Lu.

sentences.zh: 其後百六十有七年而吳有專諸之事。

translations.literal: One hundred and sixty-seven years later, in Wu there occurred the affair of Zhuan Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: One hundred and sixty-seven years later, the affair of Zhuan Zhu took place in the state of Wu.

translations.literal: After hundred sixty seven years then Wu had Zhuan Zhu affair.

sentences.zh: 專諸者，吳堂邑人也。

translations.literal: Zhuan Zhu was a man of Tangyi in Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuan Zhu was a native of Tangyi in Wu.

sentences.zh: 伍子胥之亡楚而如吳也，知專諸之能。

translations.literal: When Wu Zixu fled Chu and went to Wu, he recognized Zhuan Zhu's abilities.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Zixu fled from Chu to Wu, he recognized the abilities of Zhuan Zhu.

sentences.zh: 伍子胥既見吳王僚，說以伐楚之利。

translations.literal: After Wu Zixu had an audience with King Liao of Wu, he urged him on the advantages of attacking Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wu Zixu had gained an audience with King Liao of Wu, he pressed upon the king the advantages of launching a campaign against Chu.

sentences.zh: 吳公子光曰：「彼伍員父兄皆死於楚而員言伐楚，欲自為報私讎也，非能為吳。」

translations.literal: Prince Guang of Wu said: 'That Wu Yuan — his father and brother both died at the hands of Chu, and now he urges an attack on Chu. He seeks to avenge his own private grudge, not to serve the interests of Wu.'
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang of Wu objected, "Wu Yuan's father and elder brother both perished at the hands of Chu. His call to attack Chu serves only his private vendetta, not the interests of Wu."

sentences.zh: 吳王乃止。

translations.literal: The King of Wu thereupon dropped the matter.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wu thereupon abandoned the plan.

sentences.zh: 伍子胥知公子光之欲殺吳王僚，乃曰：「彼光將有內志，未可說以外事。」

translations.literal: Wu Zixu understood that Prince Guang wished to kill King Liao, and said: 'That prince harbors ambitions within; he cannot yet be approached about external affairs.'
translations.idiomatic: Wu Zixu perceived that Prince Guang harbored designs on King Liao's life, and remarked, "The prince nurses ambitions of his own. He cannot yet be approached about foreign ventures."

sentences.zh: 乃進專諸於公子光。

translations.literal: He then recommended Zhuan Zhu to Prince Guang.
translations.idiomatic: He then introduced Zhuan Zhu to Prince Guang.

translations.literal: Zhuan Zhu was Wu Tang yi person. Wu Zixu fled Chu went to Wu, knew Zhuan Zhu ability. Wu Zixu having seen Wu King Liao, persuaded attacking Chu benefit. Wu prince Guang said: 'That Wu Yuan father brothers all died in Chu yet Yuan speaks attack Chu, wishes self avenge private enmity, not can for Wu.' Wu king thus stopped. Wu Zixu knew Prince Guang desired kill Wu King Liao, thus said: 'That Guang will have inner ambition, cannot persuade with outer affairs.' Thus advanced Zhuan Zhu to Prince Guang.

sentences.zh: 光之父曰吳王諸樊。

translations.literal: Guang's father was King Zhu Fan of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang's father was King Zhu Fan of Wu.

sentences.zh: 諸樊弟三人：次曰餘祭，次曰夷眛，次曰季子札。

translations.literal: Zhu Fan had three younger brothers: the next was Yu Ji, then Yi Mo, then the youngest, Ji Zha.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fan had three younger brothers: Yu Ji, Yi Mo, and the youngest, Ji Zha.

sentences.zh: 諸樊知季子札賢而不立太子，以次傳三弟，欲卒致國于季子札。

translations.literal: Zhu Fan, recognizing that Ji Zha was worthy, did not establish a crown prince but passed the throne through each of his three brothers in succession, wishing ultimately to deliver the state to Ji Zha.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing Ji Zha to be a man of exceptional virtue, Zhu Fan chose not to designate an heir apparent but instead arranged for the throne to pass through each of his three brothers in turn, so that the kingdom might ultimately reach Ji Zha.

sentences.zh: 諸樊既死，傳餘祭。

translations.literal: When Zhu Fan died, the throne passed to Yu Ji.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhu Fan's death, the throne passed to Yu Ji.

sentences.zh: 餘祭死，傳夷眛。

translations.literal: When Yu Ji died, the throne passed to Yi Mo.
translations.idiomatic: When Yu Ji died, the throne passed to Yi Mo.

sentences.zh: 夷眛死，當傳季子札；

translations.literal: When Yi Mo died, the throne should have passed to Ji Zha.
translations.idiomatic: Upon Yi Mo's death, the throne should have passed to Ji Zha.

sentences.zh: 季子札逃不肯立，吳人乃立夷眛之子僚為王。

translations.literal: Ji Zha fled and refused to accept the throne, so the people of Wu installed Yi Mo's son Liao as king.
translations.idiomatic: But Ji Zha fled and refused to take the throne, so the people of Wu installed Yi Mo's son Liao as king.

sentences.zh: 公子光曰：「使以兄弟次邪，季子當立；

translations.literal: Prince Guang said: 'If succession goes by the order of brothers, then Ji Zha should be king.'
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang protested, "If the succession follows the order of brothers, then Ji Zha ought to be king."

sentences.zh: 必以子乎，則光真適嗣，當立。」

translations.literal: If it must go by sonship, then I am the true legitimate heir and should be enthroned.
translations.idiomatic: "But if it must pass to the next generation, then I, as the legitimate eldest son, am the rightful heir."

sentences.zh: 故嘗陰養謀臣以求立。

translations.literal: Therefore he secretly cultivated advisors to seek the throne.
translations.idiomatic: And so he secretly cultivated a circle of advisors in pursuit of the throne.

translations.literal: Guang father called Wu King Zhu Fan. Zhu Fan younger brothers three: next called Yu Ji, next called Yi Mei, next called Ji Zi Zha. Zhu Fan knew Ji Zi Zha worthy not established crown prince, by order passed three younger brothers, wished finally give state to Ji Zi Zha. Zhu Fan having died, passed Yu Ji. Yu Ji died, passed Yi Mei. Yi Mei died, should pass Ji Zi Zha; Ji Zi Zha fled would not establish, Wu people thus established Yi Mei son Liao as king. Prince Guang said: 'If by brothers order, Ji Zi should establish; Must by son, then Guang true proper heir, should establish.' Thus often secretly nurtured plot ministers to seek establishment.

sentences.zh: 光既得專諸，善客待之。

translations.literal: After Guang obtained Zhuan Zhu, he treated him as an honored guest.
translations.idiomatic: Once Prince Guang had secured the services of Zhuan Zhu, he treated him with the courtesy due an honored guest.

sentences.zh: 九年而楚平王死。

translations.literal: Nine years later, King Ping of Chu died.
translations.idiomatic: Nine years passed, and King Ping of Chu died.

sentences.zh: 春，吳王僚欲因楚喪，使其二弟公子蓋餘、屬庸將兵圍楚之灊；

translations.literal: In spring, King Liao of Wu wished to take advantage of Chu's mourning and sent his two brothers, Princes Gai Yu and Zhu Yong, to lead troops to besiege Qian in Chu.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring, King Liao of Wu saw an opportunity in Chu's period of mourning. He dispatched his two brothers, Princes Gai Yu and Zhu Yong, to lead an army and lay siege to Qian in Chu.

sentences.zh: 使延陵季子於晉，以觀諸侯之變。

translations.literal: He sent Yanling Ji Zha as envoy to Jin, to observe the reactions of the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: He also sent Ji Zha of Yanling on a diplomatic mission to Jin to gauge the intentions of the feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 楚發兵絕吳將蓋餘、屬庸路，吳兵不得還。

translations.literal: Chu dispatched troops to cut off the retreat of the Wu generals Gai Yu and Zhu Yong, so the Wu army could not return.
translations.idiomatic: Chu then dispatched forces to cut off the retreat of Gai Yu and Zhu Yong, stranding the Wu army with no way home.

sentences.zh: 於是公子光謂專諸曰：「此時不可失，不求何獲！

translations.literal: Thereupon Prince Guang said to Zhuan Zhu: 'This moment must not be lost — if one does not seek, what can one gain?'
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang said to Zhuan Zhu, "This is the moment — it must not be squandered. If we do not seize our chance, what will we ever gain?"

sentences.zh: 且光真王嗣，當立，季子雖來，不吾廢也。」

translations.literal: Moreover, I am the true royal heir and should be enthroned. Even if Ji Zha returns, he will not depose me.
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, I am the true royal heir and rightfully should be king. Even if Ji Zha returns, he will not unseat me."

sentences.zh: 專諸曰：「王僚可殺也。

translations.literal: Zhuan Zhu said: 'King Liao can be killed.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhuan Zhu declared, "King Liao can be killed."

sentences.zh: 母老子弱，而兩弟將兵伐楚，楚絕其後。

translations.literal: His mother is old and his sons are young, and his two brothers are leading troops against Chu, with Chu cutting off their rear.
translations.idiomatic: "His mother is old and his sons are young. His two brothers are off campaigning against Chu, and Chu has severed their line of retreat."

sentences.zh: 方今吳外困於楚，而內空無骨鯁之臣，是無如我何。」

translations.literal: At present, Wu is pressed from without by Chu and hollowed from within, with no ministers of backbone — there is nothing he can do against us.
translations.idiomatic: "Right now Wu is beset from without by Chu and left hollow within, with no ministers of stature to stand in our way. He is powerless against us."

sentences.zh: 公子光頓首曰：「光之身，子之身也。」

translations.literal: Prince Guang bowed his head to the ground and said: 'My fate is your fate.'
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang bowed low and declared, "My fate is yours."

translations.literal: Guang having obtained Zhuan Zhu, well treated as guest. Nine years then Chu Ping Wang died. Spring, Wu King Liao wished take Chu mourning, made his two younger brothers Prince Gai Yu, Zhu Yong lead troops surround Chu Qian; Sent Yanling Ji Zi to Jin, to observe feudal lords changes. Chu sent troops cut Wu generals Gai Yu, Zhu Yong road, Wu troops could not return. Thus Prince Guang told Zhuan Zhu: 'This time cannot lose, not seek what obtain! Moreover Guang true king heir, should establish, Ji Zi though come, not abolish me.' Zhuan Zhu said: 'King Liao can kill. Mother old son weak, two younger brothers lead troops attack Chu, Chu cut their rear. Now Wu externally distressed by Chu, internally empty without backbone ministers, is cannot do anything to me.' Prince Guang bowed head: 'Guang body, your body.'

sentences.zh: 四月丙子，光伏甲士於窟室中，而具酒請王僚。

translations.literal: On the bingzi day of the fourth month, Guang concealed armored soldiers in a cellar room and prepared a wine banquet to invite King Liao.
translations.idiomatic: On the bingzi day of the fourth month, Prince Guang concealed armed men in an underground chamber, then held a banquet and invited King Liao.

sentences.zh: 王僚使兵陳自宮至光之家，門戶階陛左右，皆王僚之親戚也。

translations.literal: King Liao stationed soldiers in a line from the palace all the way to Guang's residence; at the gates, stairs, and flanking positions stood Liao's own kinsmen.
translations.idiomatic: King Liao posted a cordon of soldiers stretching from the palace all the way to Prince Guang's residence. At every gate, stairway, and flanking position stood his own kinsmen.

sentences.zh: 夾立侍，皆持長鈹。

translations.literal: Attendants stood close on either side, all holding long halberds.
translations.idiomatic: Attendants flanked the path, every one of them bearing a long halberd.

sentences.zh: 酒既酣，公子光詳為足疾，入窟室中，使專諸置匕首魚炙之腹中而進之。

translations.literal: When the wine was flowing freely, Prince Guang feigned a foot ailment and withdrew into the cellar room, where he had Zhuan Zhu conceal a dagger inside the belly of a roasted fish and present it.
translations.idiomatic: When the wine had flowed freely, Prince Guang feigned a foot ailment and withdrew to the underground chamber. There, he had Zhuan Zhu conceal a dagger inside the belly of a grilled fish before presenting it to the king.

sentences.zh: 既至王前，專諸擘魚，因以匕首刺王僚，王僚立死。

translations.literal: When it reached the king, Zhuan Zhu split open the fish and with the dagger stabbed King Liao, who died instantly.
translations.idiomatic: As the dish was set before the king, Zhuan Zhu tore open the fish, seized the dagger, and plunged it into King Liao. The king died on the spot.

sentences.zh: 左右亦殺專諸，王人擾亂。

translations.literal: The attendants also killed Zhuan Zhu, and the king's men fell into chaos.
translations.idiomatic: The king's guards killed Zhuan Zhu in the same instant, and the royal retinue dissolved into chaos.

sentences.zh: 公子光出其伏甲以攻王僚之徒，盡滅之，遂自立為王，是為闔閭。

translations.literal: Prince Guang released his concealed armored soldiers to attack King Liao's followers, annihilating them completely, and then proclaimed himself king — this was King Helu.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Guang unleashed his hidden soldiers upon King Liao's followers and destroyed them to the last man. He then declared himself king — and became known as King Helu.

sentences.zh: 闔閭乃封專諸之子以為上卿。

translations.literal: King Helu enfeoffed the son of Zhuan Zhu as a high minister.
translations.idiomatic: King Helu enfeoffed Zhuan Zhu's son as a Senior Minister.

translations.literal: Fourth month bing zi, Guang hid armored soldiers in cave chamber, prepared wine invited King Liao. King Liao made troops array from palace to Guang home, doorways stairs left right, all King Liao relatives. Flanking standing attendants, all held long spears. Wine already flowing, Prince Guang feigned foot illness, entered cave chamber, made Zhuan Zhu place dagger in fish roast belly advanced it. Having reached king before, Zhuan Zhu split fish, thus with dagger stabbed King Liao, King Liao immediately died. Attendants also killed Zhuan Zhu, king people chaotic. Prince Guang brought out his hidden armored troops to attack King Liao's followers, completely exterminated them, thus self-established as king, this was Helü. Helü then enfeoffed Zhuan Zhu's son as upper minister.

sentences.zh: 其後七十餘年而晉有豫讓之事。

translations.literal: More than seventy years later, in Jin there occurred the affair of Yu Rang.
translations.idiomatic: More than seventy years afterward, the affair of Yu Rang took place in Jin.

translations.literal: After seventy plus years then Jin had Yu Rang affair.

sentences.zh: 豫讓者，晉人也，故嘗事范氏及中行氏，而無所知名。

translations.literal: Yu Rang was a man of Jin who had formerly served the Fan and Zhonghang clans, but gained no recognition.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang was a man of Jin. He had formerly served the Fan and Zhonghang clans, but achieved no distinction.

sentences.zh: 去而事智伯，智伯甚尊寵之。

translations.literal: He left and entered the service of Zhi Bo, who honored and favored him greatly.
translations.idiomatic: He left their service and entered that of Zhi Bo, who treated him with great honor and favor.

sentences.zh: 及智伯伐趙襄子，趙襄子與韓、魏合謀滅智伯，滅智伯之後而三分其地。

translations.literal: When Zhi Bo attacked Zhao Xiangzi, Zhao Xiangzi joined with Han and Wei in a plot to destroy Zhi Bo, and after his destruction they divided his territory three ways.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhi Bo launched an attack against Zhao Xiangzi, Zhao Xiangzi conspired with the houses of Han and Wei to destroy him. After Zhi Bo's annihilation, they divided his territories among themselves.

sentences.zh: 趙襄子最怨智伯，漆其頭以為飲器。

translations.literal: Zhao Xiangzi bore the deepest grudge against Zhi Bo, and lacquered his skull to use as a drinking vessel.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xiangzi harbored the deepest enmity toward Zhi Bo, and had his skull lacquered to serve as a drinking cup.

sentences.zh: 豫讓遁逃山中，曰：「嗟乎！

translations.literal: Yu Rang fled and hid in the mountains, saying: 'Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang fled into the mountains and cried out, "Alas!"

sentences.zh: 士為知己者死，女為說己者容。

translations.literal: A man of honor dies for one who understands him, as a woman adorns herself for one who delights in her.
translations.idiomatic: "A true man dies for one who truly knows him, just as a woman makes herself beautiful for one who delights in her."

sentences.zh: 今智伯知我，我必為報讎而死，以報智伯，則吾魂魄不愧矣。」

translations.literal: Now Zhi Bo understood me, and I must avenge him even at the cost of my life, to repay Zhi Bo — then my spirit will bear no shame.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhi Bo understood my worth. I must avenge him, even at the cost of my own life, to repay his recognition — then my spirit may rest without shame."

sentences.zh: 乃變名姓為刑人，入宮涂廁，中挾匕首，欲以刺襄子。

translations.literal: He then changed his name and took the guise of a convict, entered the palace to plaster the privy, and concealed a dagger on his person, intending to assassinate Zhao Xiangzi.
translations.idiomatic: He changed his name, disguised himself as a convict laborer, and entered the palace to plaster the privy walls, concealing a dagger on his person with the intention of assassinating Zhao Xiangzi.

sentences.zh: 襄子如廁，心動，執問涂廁之刑人，則豫讓，內持刀兵，曰：「欲為智伯報仇！」

translations.literal: When Xiangzi went to the privy, he felt a premonition; he seized and questioned the convict plastering the privy — it was Yu Rang, who had a blade concealed on him, and he said: 'I wish to avenge Zhi Bo!'
translations.idiomatic: When Xiangzi entered the privy, his heart stirred with unease. He seized the convict laborer and questioned him — it was Yu Rang, who had a blade hidden on his person. Yu Rang declared, "I seek to avenge Zhi Bo!"

sentences.zh: 左右欲誅之。

translations.literal: The attendants wished to execute him.
translations.idiomatic: The attendants moved to execute him on the spot.

sentences.zh: 襄子曰：「彼義人也，吾謹避之耳。

translations.literal: Xiangzi said: 'He is a man of righteousness. I shall simply take care to avoid him.'
translations.idiomatic: Xiangzi stayed them, saying, "This is a man of honor. I need only be careful to avoid him."

sentences.zh: 且智伯亡無後，而其臣欲為報仇，此天下之賢人也。」

translations.literal: Moreover, Zhi Bo is dead with no descendants, yet his retainer seeks to avenge him — this is a worthy man of all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhi Bo is dead and left no heir, yet his retainer still seeks to avenge him. Such a man is the most worthy in all under Heaven."

sentences.zh: 卒醳去之。

translations.literal: In the end he pardoned and released him.
translations.idiomatic: And so he pardoned Yu Rang and set him free.

translations.literal: Yu Rang was Jin person, formerly served Fan clan and Zhonghang clan, without known reputation. Left served Zhi Bo, Zhi Bo much honored favored him. When Zhi Bo attacked Zhao Xiangzi, Zhao Xiangzi with Han, Wei united plotted exterminate Zhi Bo, exterminated Zhi Bo after divided his land three ways. Zhao Xiangzi most resented Zhi Bo, lacquered his head to make drinking vessel. Yu Rang fled hid in mountains, said: 'Alas! Scholar dies for one who knows him, woman adorns for one who pleases her. Now Zhi Bo knows me, I must avenge enmity die, to repay Zhi Bo, then my soul not ashamed.' Thus changed name surname became criminal, entered palace smeared toilets, middle hid dagger, wished stab Xiangzi. Xiangzi went to toilet, heart moved, seized questioned toilet smearing criminal, was Yu Rang, inside held blade weapons, said: 'Wish avenge Zhi Bo!' Attendants wished execute him. Xiangzi said: 'He righteous person, I carefully avoid him. Moreover Zhi Bo died without descendants, his minister wishes avenge enmity, this world's worthy person.' Ultimately released departed him.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，豫讓又漆身為厲，吞炭為啞，使形狀不可知，行乞於市。

translations.literal: After some time, Yu Rang coated his body with lacquer to raise sores, and swallowed charcoal to make himself mute, so as to alter his appearance beyond recognition, and went begging in the marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, Yu Rang smeared his body with lacquer to raise disfiguring sores and swallowed hot charcoal to destroy his voice, rendering himself unrecognizable. He then went begging through the marketplace.

sentences.zh: 其妻不識也。

translations.literal: His own wife did not recognize him.
translations.idiomatic: Even his own wife failed to recognize him.

sentences.zh: 行見其友，其友識之，曰：「汝非豫讓邪？」

translations.literal: Walking along, he encountered a friend, and the friend recognized him, saying: 'Are you not Yu Rang?'
translations.idiomatic: As he wandered the streets, he encountered a friend who saw through his disguise and asked, "Are you not Yu Rang?"

sentences.zh: 曰：「我是也。」

translations.literal: He replied: 'I am.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang answered, "I am."

sentences.zh: 其友為泣曰：「以子之才，委質而臣事襄子，襄子必近幸子。

translations.literal: His friend wept for him and said: 'With your talents, if you were to pledge yourself as a subject to Xiangzi, he would surely take you into his confidence.'
translations.idiomatic: His friend wept and said, "With your talents, if you pledged yourself to Xiangzi as his servant, he would surely draw you close and trust you."

sentences.zh: 近幸子，乃為所欲，顧不易邪？

translations.literal: Once he trusts you, then you could do as you wish. Would that not be easier?
translations.idiomatic: "Once in his confidence, you could accomplish whatever you wished. Would that not be the easier path?"

sentences.zh: 何乃殘身苦形，欲以求報襄子，不亦難乎！」

translations.literal: Why mutilate your body and ruin your form in pursuit of vengeance against Xiangzi? Is this not the hardest path?
translations.idiomatic: "Why ravage your own body and destroy your appearance to pursue this vendetta against Xiangzi? Is that not an impossibly difficult course?"

sentences.zh: 豫讓曰：「既已委質臣事人，而求殺之，是懷二心以事其君也。

translations.literal: Yu Rang said: 'If I were already a subject serving someone and then sought to kill him, that would be to serve my lord with a divided heart.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang replied, "If I were to pledge myself as another man's servant and then seek to kill him, that would be serving my lord with treachery in my heart."

sentences.zh: 且吾所為者極難耳！

translations.literal: Besides, what I pursue is the most difficult path of all!
translations.idiomatic: "What I pursue is indeed the most difficult path of all!"

sentences.zh: 然所以為此者，將以愧天下後世之為人臣懷二心以事其君者也。」

translations.literal: Yet the reason I do this is to shame all those in future generations who serve their lords with divided hearts.
translations.idiomatic: "Yet I choose this path so that all who come after — every minister who would serve his lord with a divided heart — may be shamed by my example."

translations.literal: Dwelt moment, Yu Rang again lacquered body became leper, swallowed charcoal became mute, made appearance unrecognizable, begged in market. His wife not recognized him. Walking saw his friend, friend recognized him, said: 'You not Yu Rang?' Said: 'I am.' Friend wept for him: 'With your talent, submit serve Xiangzi, Xiangzi must approach favor you. Approach favor you, then accomplish what desired, moreover not easy? Why thus harm body bitter form, wish seek avenge Xiangzi, not difficult!' Yu Rang said: 'Already submitted served person, yet seek kill him, is harbor two hearts serve lord. Moreover what I do extremely difficult! Yet reason do this, will shame world later generations ministers harbor two hearts serve lord.'

sentences.zh: 既去，頃之，襄子當出，豫讓伏於所當過之橋下。

translations.literal: After he left, before long, Xiangzi was about to go out, and Yu Rang lay in wait beneath a bridge along his route.
translations.idiomatic: After they parted, it was not long before Xiangzi prepared to go out. Yu Rang concealed himself beneath a bridge along the route the lord was sure to take.

sentences.zh: 襄子至橋，馬驚，襄子曰：「此必是豫讓也。」

translations.literal: When Xiangzi reached the bridge, his horse shied. Xiangzi said: 'This must be Yu Rang.'
translations.idiomatic: When Xiangzi reached the bridge, his horse reared in alarm. "This must be Yu Rang," Xiangzi said.

sentences.zh: 使人問之，果豫讓也。

translations.literal: He sent men to investigate, and indeed it was Yu Rang.
translations.idiomatic: He sent men to search, and indeed it was Yu Rang.

sentences.zh: 於是襄子乃數豫讓曰：「子不嘗事范、中行氏乎？

translations.literal: Thereupon Xiangzi rebuked Yu Rang, saying: 'Did you not once serve the Fan and Zhonghang clans?'
translations.idiomatic: Xiangzi confronted Yu Rang and demanded, "Did you not once serve the Fan and Zhonghang clans?"

sentences.zh: 智伯盡滅之，而子不為報讎，而反委質臣於智伯。

translations.literal: Zhi Bo destroyed them completely, yet you did not seek to avenge them, but instead pledged yourself to Zhi Bo.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhi Bo annihilated them utterly, yet you never sought vengeance on their behalf. Instead, you pledged yourself to Zhi Bo's service."

sentences.zh: 智伯亦已死矣，而子獨何以為之報讎之深也？」

translations.literal: Zhi Bo too is now dead, yet you alone — why do you pursue vengeance for him so deeply?
translations.idiomatic: "Zhi Bo is now dead as well. Why, then, do you pursue vengeance for him alone, and with such relentless devotion?"

sentences.zh: 豫讓曰：「臣事范、中行氏，范、中行氏皆眾人遇我，我故眾人報之。

translations.literal: Yu Rang said: 'When I served the Fan and Zhonghang clans, they treated me as an ordinary man, so I repaid them as an ordinary man.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang answered, "When I served the Fan and Zhonghang clans, they treated me as a common man, and so I repaid them as a common man."

sentences.zh: 至於智伯，國士遇我，我故國士報之。」

translations.literal: As for Zhi Bo, he treated me as a man of the highest caliber, and so I repay him as a man of the highest caliber.
translations.idiomatic: "But Zhi Bo treated me as a man of the highest caliber — a hero of the realm — and so I must repay him as such."

sentences.zh: 襄子喟然嘆息而泣曰：「嗟乎豫子！

translations.literal: Xiangzi sighed deeply and wept, saying: 'Alas, Yu Rang!'
translations.idiomatic: Xiangzi heaved a deep sigh, and tears fell from his eyes. "Alas, Yu Rang!" he cried.

sentences.zh: 子之為智伯，名既成矣，而寡人赦子，亦已足矣。

translations.literal: Your devotion to Zhi Bo has already made your name. And my pardoning of you has also been sufficient.
translations.idiomatic: "Your devotion to Zhi Bo has already won you an immortal name. And I have pardoned you once — that should suffice."

sentences.zh: 子其自為計，寡人不復釋子！」

translations.literal: Make your own plans. I shall not release you again!
translations.idiomatic: "Make whatever arrangements you must. I will not release you again."

sentences.zh: 使兵圍之。

translations.literal: He ordered soldiers to surround him.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered his soldiers to surround Yu Rang.

sentences.zh: 豫讓曰：「臣聞明主不掩人之美，而忠臣有死名之義。

translations.literal: Yu Rang said: 'I have heard that an enlightened lord does not conceal a man's virtue, and a loyal minister is duty-bound to die for his name.'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang spoke: "I have heard that a wise lord does not obscure a man's virtue, and a loyal minister embraces death to fulfill his duty."

sentences.zh: 前君已寬赦臣，天下莫不稱君之賢。

translations.literal: Previously, my lord generously pardoned me, and all under Heaven praised his worthiness.
translations.idiomatic: "Before, my lord pardoned me with great magnanimity, and all under Heaven praised your worthiness."

sentences.zh: 今日之事，臣固伏誅，然願請君之衣而擊之，焉以致報讎之意，則雖死不恨。

translations.literal: In today's affair, I am resolved to accept my punishment. But I beg to strike my lord's robes, that I may fulfill the intent of vengeance — then even in death I shall have no regret.
translations.idiomatic: "Today I accept my punishment. But I beg you — let me strike your robes, that I may at least fulfill the spirit of my vengeance. Then I shall die without regret."

sentences.zh: 非所敢望也，敢布腹心！」

translations.literal: This is not something I dare hope for, but I venture to lay bare my heart!
translations.idiomatic: "I dare not hope for such a thing, yet I lay bare my heart before you!"

sentences.zh: 於是襄子大義之，乃使使持衣與豫讓。

translations.literal: Thereupon Xiangzi, deeply moved by his righteousness, had an attendant bring his robes to Yu Rang.
translations.idiomatic: Deeply moved by his sense of honor, Xiangzi ordered an attendant to bring his robes and present them to Yu Rang.

sentences.zh: 豫讓拔劍三躍而擊之，曰：「吾可以下報智伯矣！」

translations.literal: Yu Rang drew his sword, leaped three times and struck the robes, crying: 'Now I may go below and report to Zhi Bo!'
translations.idiomatic: Yu Rang drew his sword, leaped three times, and slashed at the robes. "Now I may descend to the world below and face Zhi Bo!" he cried.

sentences.zh: 遂伏劍自殺。

translations.literal: He then fell upon his sword and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Then he fell upon his sword and died.

sentences.zh: 死之日，趙國志士聞之，皆為涕泣。

translations.literal: On the day of his death, every man of honor in the state of Zhao who heard the news wept for him.
translations.idiomatic: On the day of his death, when word reached the men of honor throughout the state of Zhao, every one of them wept.

translations.literal: Having left, moment, Xiangzi when exit, Yu Rang hid at bridge he must pass. Xiangzi reached bridge, horse startled, Xiangzi said: 'This must Yu Rang.' Made people question, indeed Yu Rang. Thus Xiangzi numbered Yu Rang: 'You not formerly served Fan, Zhonghang clans? Zhi Bo completely exterminated them, yet you not avenge enmity, instead submitted ministered Zhi Bo. Zhi Bo also died, yet you alone why avenge so deep?' Yu Rang said: 'Minister served Fan, Zhonghang clans, Fan, Zhonghang clans all common people treated me, I thus common people repay. As for Zhi Bo, state knight treated me, I thus state knight repay.' Xiangzi sighed deeply wept: 'Alas Yu child! You for Zhi Bo, name already completed, my pardon you also sufficient. You self-plan, I no longer release you!' Made troops surround him. Yu Rang said: 'I heard enlightened lord not conceal others' beauty, loyal minister has die for fame righteousness. Former lord already generously pardoned minister, world none not praise lord's worthiness. Today matter, minister certainly submit to execution, yet wish request lord's robe strike it, thus realize avenge intention, then though die not regret. Not dare hope, dare bare heartfelt!' Thus Xiangzi greatly considered righteous, made envoy hold robe give Yu Rang. Yu Rang drew sword three leaps struck it, said: 'I can below repay Zhi Bo!' Thus fell sword suicide. Died day, Zhao state righteous scholars heard, all wept for him.

sentences.zh: 其後四十餘年而軹有聶政之事。

translations.literal: More than forty years later, in Zhi there occurred the affair of Nie Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: More than forty years afterward, the affair of Nie Zheng took place in Zhi.

translations.literal: After forty plus years then Zhi had Nie Zheng affair.

sentences.zh: 聶政者，軹深井里人也。

translations.literal: Nie Zheng was a man of Shenjing ward in Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng was from the Shenjing neighborhood of Zhi.

sentences.zh: 殺人避仇，與母、姊如齊，以屠為事。

translations.literal: He killed a man and fled to avoid the vendetta, living with his mother and elder sister in Qi, where he worked as a butcher.
translations.idiomatic: Having killed a man, he fled with his mother and elder sister to Qi to escape retaliation, and there took up the trade of a butcher.

translations.literal: Nie Zheng was Zhi Shenjing li person. Killed person avoided enmity, with mother, sister went to Qi, took butchering as occupation.

sentences.zh: 久之，濮陽嚴仲子事韓哀侯，與韓相俠累有卻。

translations.literal: After a long time, Yan Zhongzi of Puyang, who served Marquis Ai of Han, had a falling out with the Han minister Xia Lei.
translations.idiomatic: Some time later, Yan Zhongzi of Puyang, a retainer of Marquis Ai of Han, developed a bitter feud with Xia Lei, the prime minister of Han.

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子恐誅，亡去，游求人可以報俠累者。

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi, fearing execution, fled and traveled widely seeking someone who could take revenge on Xia Lei.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing for his life, Yan Zhongzi fled and traveled far and wide in search of someone capable of taking revenge on Xia Lei.

sentences.zh: 至齊，齊人或言聶政勇敢士也，避仇隱於屠者之閒。

translations.literal: When he reached Qi, someone in Qi told him that Nie Zheng was a man of brave daring who was hiding among the butchers to avoid a vendetta.
translations.idiomatic: When he arrived in Qi, someone told him that Nie Zheng was a man of extraordinary courage who was hiding among the butchers to escape a blood feud.

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子至門請，數反，然後具酒自暢聶政母前。

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi went to his door to call on him, visiting repeatedly, and then prepared a feast of wine before Nie Zheng's mother.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Zhongzi went to Nie Zheng's door to pay his respects, returning several times before at last hosting a banquet of wine in the presence of Nie Zheng's mother.

sentences.zh: 酒酣，嚴仲子奉黃金百溢，前為聶政母壽。

translations.literal: When the wine had flowed freely, Yan Zhongzi presented a hundred measures of gold and offered them as a birthday gift for Nie Zheng's mother.
translations.idiomatic: When the wine flowed freely, Yan Zhongzi presented a hundred measures of gold, offering them as a birthday gift to Nie Zheng's mother.

sentences.zh: 聶政驚怪其厚，固謝嚴仲子。

translations.literal: Nie Zheng was astonished at such generosity and firmly declined Yan Zhongzi's gift.
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng was startled by the extravagance and firmly refused the gift.

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子固進，而聶政謝曰：「臣幸有老母，家貧，客游以為狗屠，可以旦夕得甘毳以養親。

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi pressed his offer, but Nie Zheng declined, saying: 'I am fortunate to still have my aged mother. Though our family is poor, I wander as a guest and work as a dog butcher, enough to provide her with delicacies morning and evening.'
translations.idiomatic: Yan Zhongzi insisted, but Nie Zheng demurred: "I am fortunate that my aged mother is still alive. Though our family is poor, I wander far and work as a butcher — just enough to provide her with delicacies morning and evening."

sentences.zh: 親供養備，不敢當仲子之賜。」

translations.literal: Her care and sustenance are fully provided for; I dare not accept your gift.
translations.idiomatic: "Her needs are fully met. I cannot accept such a gift."

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子辟人，因為聶政言曰：「臣有仇，而行游諸侯眾矣；

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi dismissed the others, and spoke privately to Nie Zheng: 'I have an enemy. I have traveled through many states.'
translations.idiomatic: Yan Zhongzi cleared the room and spoke to Nie Zheng privately: "I have an enemy, and I have wandered through many states in search of help."

sentences.zh: 然至齊，竊聞足下義甚高，故進百金者，將用為大人麤糲之費，得以交足下之驩，豈敢以有求望邪！」

translations.literal: But when I came to Qi and heard of your exceptional sense of honor, I present this hundred gold — not expecting anything — but merely to cover the rough expenses of your household and to gain your friendship. How would I dare seek anything in return?
translations.idiomatic: "When I arrived in Qi and learned of your lofty sense of honor, I offered this gold not with any expectation, but merely to defray your household expenses and to secure the pleasure of your friendship. How could I dare expect anything in return?"

sentences.zh: 聶政曰：「臣所以降志辱身居市井屠者，徒幸以養老母；

translations.literal: Nie Zheng said: 'The reason I have lowered my ambitions and endured disgrace, living among the market butchers, is solely for the good fortune of supporting my aged mother.'
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng said, "The reason I have swallowed my pride and endured a humble life among the market butchers is solely to care for my aged mother."

sentences.zh: 老母在，政身未敢以許人也。」

translations.literal: While my mother lives, I dare not pledge my life to another.
translations.idiomatic: "While my mother lives, I dare not pledge my life to any man's cause."

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子固讓，聶政竟不肯受也。

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi pressed again, but Nie Zheng in the end refused to accept.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Zhongzi pressed him once more, but Nie Zheng remained resolute in his refusal.

sentences.zh: 然嚴仲子卒備賓主之禮而去。

translations.literal: Nevertheless, Yan Zhongzi fulfilled all the courtesies of a host and departed.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Yan Zhongzi observed all the courtesies of a proper host before taking his leave.

translations.literal: Long time, Puyang Yan Zhongzi served Han Ai Hou, with Han minister Xia Lei had grudge. Yan Zhongzi feared execution, fled departed, traveled sought person can avenge Xia Lei. Reached Qi, Qi people some spoke Nie Zheng brave warrior, avoided enmity hid among butchers. Yan Zhongzi reached door requested, several times returned, then prepared wine self-toasted before Nie Zheng mother. Wine flowing, Yan Zhongzi offered yellow gold hundred yi, presented Nie Zheng mother longevity. Nie Zheng startled strange his thickness, firmly declined Yan Zhongzi. Yan Zhongzi firmly advanced, Nie Zheng declined said: 'Minister fortunately have aged mother, family poor, guest travel as dog butcher, can morning evening obtain sweet broth nourish parents. Parents nourishment complete, dare not accept Zhongzi's gift.' Yan Zhongzi dismissed people, thus spoke Nie Zheng: 'Minister have enmity, traveled feudal lords many; Yet reached Qi, secretly heard your righteousness very high, thus advanced hundred jin gold, will use as adult coarse food expense, able befriend your joy, how dare have request expectation!' Nie Zheng said: 'Minister reason lower ambition humble body dwell market butcher, merely fortunately nourish aged mother; Aged mother in, Zheng body not dare promise others.' Yan Zhongzi firmly urged, Nie Zheng ultimately would not accept. Yet Yan Zhongzi completed host guest ritual departed.

sentences.zh: 久之，聶政母死。

translations.literal: A long time later, Nie Zheng's mother died.
translations.idiomatic: A long time passed, and then Nie Zheng's mother died.

sentences.zh: 既已葬，除服，聶政曰：「嗟乎！

translations.literal: After she had been buried and the mourning period had ended, Nie Zheng said: 'Alas!'
translations.idiomatic: After the burial and the completion of the mourning period, Nie Zheng said, "Alas!"

sentences.zh: 政乃市井之人，鼓刀以屠；

translations.literal: I am but a man of the marketplace, a butcher who wields the knife.
translations.idiomatic: "I am nothing but a man of the marketplace, wielding a butcher's knife."

sentences.zh: 而嚴仲子乃諸侯之卿相也，不遠千里，枉車騎而交臣。

translations.literal: Yet Yan Zhongzi is a minister of a feudal state; he did not disdain the distance of a thousand li, but came with his carriage to befriend me.
translations.idiomatic: "Yet Yan Zhongzi, a minister to feudal lords, thought nothing of traveling a thousand li to extend his friendship to me."

sentences.zh: 臣之所以待之，至淺鮮矣，未有大功可以稱者，而嚴仲子奉百金為親壽，我雖不受，然是者徒深知政也。

translations.literal: How shallowly I repaid him! I have accomplished no great deed to deserve such kindness, yet Yan Zhongzi presented a hundred gold for my mother's birthday. Though I did not accept, his understanding of me runs deep.
translations.idiomatic: "How meager was my response! I performed no great service worthy of his regard, yet Yan Zhongzi offered a hundred measures of gold for my mother's birthday. Though I refused the gift, his understanding of my character runs uncommonly deep."

sentences.zh: 夫賢者以感忿睚眦之意，而親信窮僻之人，而政獨安得嘿然而已乎！

translations.literal: A worthy man who feels the sting of resentment and places his trust in a humble and obscure person — can I, Zheng, remain silent and do nothing?
translations.idiomatic: "When a man of worth, burning with righteous indignation, places his trust in someone as obscure and lowly as myself — how can I simply remain idle and silent?"

sentences.zh: 且前日要政，政徒以老母；

translations.literal: Moreover, when he first approached me, I declined only because my mother was still alive.
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, when he first sought my aid, I declined only because my mother was still alive."

sentences.zh: 老母今以天年終，政將為知己者用。」

translations.literal: Now my mother has lived out her natural years and died. I shall put myself to use for one who truly knows me.
translations.idiomatic: "Now my mother has lived a full life and passed away. I shall devote myself to the service of one who truly knows me."

sentences.zh: 乃遂西至濮陽，見嚴仲子曰：「前日所以不許仲子者，徒以親在；

translations.literal: He then traveled west to Puyang, where he called on Yan Zhongzi and said: 'The reason I refused you before was solely because my parents were alive.'
translations.idiomatic: He traveled westward to Puyang, called on Yan Zhongzi, and said, "The reason I refused you before was solely because my mother was still alive."

sentences.zh: 今不幸而母以天年終。

translations.literal: Now, sadly, my mother has lived out her natural years and passed away.
translations.idiomatic: "Now, alas, my mother has lived out her natural span and departed this world."

sentences.zh: 仲子所欲報仇者為誰？

translations.literal: Who is the person you wish to take revenge upon?
translations.idiomatic: "Who is the man you wish to take vengeance upon?"

sentences.zh: 請得從事焉！」

translations.literal: I ask to be allowed to act!
translations.idiomatic: "Allow me to act."

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子具告曰：「臣之仇韓相俠累，俠累又韓君之季父也，宗族盛多，居處兵衛甚設，臣欲使人刺之，（眾）終莫能就。

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi told him the full story: 'My enemy is Xia Lei, the prime minister of Han. Xia Lei is also an uncle of the Han ruler. His clan is large and powerful, and he is heavily guarded. I have tried to send men to assassinate him, but none has succeeded.'
translations.idiomatic: Yan Zhongzi laid out the full story: "My enemy is Xia Lei, the prime minister of Han. He is also an uncle of the ruler of Han. His clan is vast and powerful, and his residence bristles with guards. Every man I have sent to assassinate him has failed."

sentences.zh: 今足下幸而不棄，請益其車騎壯士可為足下輔翼者。」

translations.literal: Now if you will not refuse me, I shall provide you with additional chariots and warriors to serve as your support.
translations.idiomatic: "Now that you have consented, allow me to furnish you with chariots and warriors to support you."

sentences.zh: 聶政曰：「韓之與衛，相去中閒不甚遠，今殺人之相，相又國君之親，此其勢不可以多人，多人不能無生得失，生得失則語泄，語泄是韓舉國而與仲子為讎，豈不殆哉！」

translations.literal: Nie Zheng said: 'The distance between Han and Wei is not very great. Now to kill the prime minister of a state — a minister who is kin to the ruler — this cannot be done with many men. With many men, there will inevitably be mishaps; with mishaps, word will leak; with leaks, all of Han will rise against you. Would that not be perilous?'
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng replied, "Han and Wei are not far apart. Now, to assassinate a state's prime minister — a man who is kin to the ruler — this cannot be done with a crowd. With too many people, something will go wrong. If something goes wrong, word will leak out. And if word leaks, all of Han will rise against you as an enemy. Would that not be catastrophic?"

sentences.zh: 遂謝車騎人徒，聶政乃辭獨行。

translations.literal: He declined the chariots, horsemen, and attendants, and Nie Zheng departed alone.
translations.idiomatic: He declined the offer of chariots, horsemen, and attendants. Nie Zheng set out alone.

translations.literal: Long time, Nie Zheng mother died. Already buried, removed mourning, Nie Zheng said: 'Alas! Zheng is market person, beats knife butchers; Yan Zhongzi is feudal lords' minister, not far thousand li, bent chariot rode befriend minister. Minister treated him, extremely shallow few, not have great merit can match, yet Yan Zhongzi offered hundred jin gold as parents longevity. I though not accept, yet this merely deeply knows Zheng. Worthy person with moved resent glare intention, personally trust poor remote person, yet Zheng alone how can silently already! Moreover previous invited Zheng, Zheng merely because aged mother; Aged mother now heaven years ended, Zheng will for knower use.' Thus west reached Puyang, saw Yan Zhongzi said: 'Previous not permitted Zhongzi, merely because parents in; Now unfortunately mother heaven years ended. Zhongzi desired avenge enmity for who? Please able follow affairs!' Yan Zhongzi completely told: 'Minister's enmity Han minister Xia Lei, Xia Lei again Han lord's younger uncle, clan numerous, dwelling guard weapons very equipped, minister wished make person stab him, finally none could succeed. Now you fortunately not abandon, please increase chariot riders strong warriors can be your assistants.' Nie Zheng said: 'Han and Wei, distance between not very far, now kill person's minister, minister again state lord's relative, this situation cannot many people, many people cannot without alive captured lost, alive captured lost then words leak, words leak is Han whole state with Zhongzi as enmity, how not dangerous!' Thus declined chariot riders people followers, Nie Zheng then alone departed.

sentences.zh: 杖劍至韓，韓相俠累方坐府上，持兵戟而衛侍者甚眾。

translations.literal: Carrying a sword, he reached Han. The prime minister Xia Lei was sitting in his office, surrounded by guards bearing weapons and halberds.
translations.idiomatic: Carrying a sword, he made his way to Han. The prime minister Xia Lei was seated in his ministerial hall, surrounded by a throng of guards bearing weapons and halberds.

sentences.zh: 聶政直入，上階刺殺俠累，左右大亂。

translations.literal: Nie Zheng walked straight in, ascended the stairs, and stabbed Xia Lei to death. The attendants fell into great confusion.
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng strode straight through them, mounted the stairs, and stabbed Xia Lei to death. The guards erupted in pandemonium.

sentences.zh: 聶政大呼，所擊殺者數十人，因自皮面決眼，自屠出腸，遂以死。

translations.literal: Nie Zheng let out a great shout and struck down several dozen men, then flayed the skin from his own face, gouged out his eyes, cut open his belly and pulled out his entrails, and died.
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng let out a mighty roar and cut down dozens of men. Then he flayed the skin from his own face, gouged out his eyes, sliced open his belly, and tore out his entrails before dying.

translations.literal: Held sword reached Han, Han minister Xia Lei just sat office above, held weapons halberds while guard attendants very many. Nie Zheng straight entered, ascended stairs stabbed killed Xia Lei, left right greatly chaotic. Nie Zheng greatly called, those he struck killed several ten persons, thus self-flayed face gouged eyes, self-butcher disembowel intestines, thus died.

sentences.zh: 韓取聶政尸暴於市，購問莫知誰子。

translations.literal: Han placed Nie Zheng's body on display in the marketplace and offered a reward, but no one knew whose son he was.
translations.idiomatic: The men of Han placed Nie Zheng's body on public display in the marketplace and posted a reward, but no one could identify him.

sentences.zh: 於是韓（購）縣［購］之，有能言殺相俠累者予千金。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han posted a public bounty: whoever could identify the man who killed Prime Minister Xia Lei would receive a thousand pieces of gold.
translations.idiomatic: Han then publicly announced a bounty: a thousand pieces of gold to anyone who could name the man who had killed Prime Minister Xia Lei.

sentences.zh: 久之莫知也。

translations.literal: For a long time, no one came forward.
translations.idiomatic: For a long time, no one came forward.

translations.literal: Han took Nie Zheng corpse exposed market, sought questioned none knew whose son. Thus Han offered reward, able speak killed minister Xia Lei give thousand jin gold. Long time none knew.

sentences.zh: 政姊榮聞人有刺殺韓相者，賊不得，國不知其名姓，暴其尸而縣之千金，乃於邑曰：「其是吾弟與？

translations.literal: Nie Zheng's elder sister Rong heard that someone had assassinated the prime minister of Han, that the killer remained unidentified, his body exposed with a bounty of a thousand gold offered, and she cried out in grief: 'Could it be my brother?'
translations.idiomatic: Nie Zheng's elder sister Rong heard that someone had assassinated the prime minister of Han — that the killer's identity remained unknown, his body displayed publicly with a bounty of a thousand gold pieces — and she cried out in anguish, "Could this be my brother?"

sentences.zh: 嗟乎，嚴仲子知吾弟！」

translations.literal: Alas! Yan Zhongzi understood my brother!
translations.idiomatic: "Alas! It was Yan Zhongzi who truly understood my brother!"

sentences.zh: 立起，如韓，之市，而死者果政也，伏尸哭極哀，曰：「是軹深井里所謂聶政者也。」

translations.literal: She rose at once and traveled to Han, went to the marketplace, and found that the dead man was indeed Nie Zheng. She threw herself upon his body, wailing with the most bitter grief, and said: 'This is the man known as Nie Zheng of the Shenjing neighborhood of Zhi.'
translations.idiomatic: She set out at once for Han. When she reached the marketplace and saw the body, it was indeed Nie Zheng. She threw herself upon his corpse, weeping with the most wrenching grief, and cried, "This is the man known as Nie Zheng of the Shenjing neighborhood of Zhi."

sentences.zh: 市行者諸眾人皆曰：「此人暴虐吾國相，王縣購其名姓千金，夫人不聞與？

translations.literal: All the passersby and people in the market said: 'This man committed a violent crime against our state's prime minister, and the king has posted a bounty of a thousand gold for his identity. Has this woman not heard?'
translations.idiomatic: The crowd of onlookers in the marketplace said, "This man committed an act of violence against our prime minister, and the king has posted a bounty of a thousand gold for his identity. Has this woman not heard?"

sentences.zh: 何敢來識之也？」

translations.literal: How does she dare come to identify him?
translations.idiomatic: "How does she dare come forward to identify him?"

sentences.zh: 榮應之曰：「聞之。

translations.literal: Rong replied: 'I have heard.'
translations.idiomatic: Rong answered, "I have heard."

sentences.zh: 然政所以蒙污辱自棄於市販之閒者，為老母幸無恙，妾未嫁也。

translations.literal: But the reason Nie Zheng bore the humiliation and degraded himself to live among the market butchers was that our aged mother was still well, and I was not yet married.
translations.idiomatic: "The reason Nie Zheng endured humiliation and debased himself to live among the butchers was that our aged mother was still alive and I was not yet married."

sentences.zh: 親既以天年下世，妾已嫁夫，嚴仲子乃察舉吾弟困污之中而交之，澤厚矣，可柰何！

translations.literal: Now that our mother has passed in the fullness of her years, and I am already wed, Yan Zhongzi singled out my brother from the midst of poverty and befriended him — his kindness was immense. What could my brother do but repay it?
translations.idiomatic: "Now our mother has died in the fullness of her years, and I am already wed. Yan Zhongzi raised my brother from poverty and obscurity and extended his friendship — such kindness was profound beyond measure. What choice did my brother have but to repay it?"

sentences.zh: 士固為知己者死，今乃以妾尚在之故，重自刑以絕從，妾其柰何畏歿身之誅，終滅賢弟之名！」

translations.literal: A true man of honor dies for one who understands him. Now, only because I am still alive, he has mutilated himself beyond recognition to prevent anyone from tracing him back to me. How can I, out of fear for my own life, allow the name of my worthy brother to perish?
translations.idiomatic: "A man of true honor will die for one who understands him. It was only because I was still alive that he disfigured himself to sever any connection to me. How can I, out of fear of death, allow the name of my noble brother to vanish into oblivion?"

sentences.zh: 大驚韓市人。

translations.literal: She greatly shocked the people in the Han marketplace.
translations.idiomatic: Her words stunned the people in the marketplace of Han.

sentences.zh: 乃大呼天者三，卒於邑悲哀而死政之旁。

translations.literal: She cried out to Heaven three times, then collapsed in grief and sorrow and died beside Nie Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: She cried out to Heaven three times, then collapsed in a paroxysm of grief and died beside her brother.

translations.literal: Zheng sister Rong heard had person stabbed killed Han minister, thief not obtained, state not know his name surname, exposed his corpse offered thousand jin gold, thus in sorrow said: 'Perhaps my younger brother? Alas, Yan Zhongzi knows my brother!' Immediately rose, went Han, market, dead person indeed Zheng, prostrated corpse wept extreme sorrow, said: 'This Zhi Shenjing li called Nie Zheng.' Market walkers various people all said: 'This person violently abused our state minister, king offered reward his name surname thousand jin gold, lady not hear? Why dare come identify him?' Rong responded said: 'Heard. Yet Zheng reason bore defilement disgrace self-abandoned in market trading between, for aged mother fortunately without illness, concubine not married. Parents already heaven years descended world, concubine already married husband, Yan Zhongzi then examined promoted my brother poverty defilement middle befriended him, favor thick, how can! Scholar certainly dies for knower, now because concubine still in reason, heavy self-punished to cut followers, concubine how fear death body punishment, finally extinguish worthy brother name!' Greatly startled Han market people. Thus greatly called heaven three times, finally in sorrow sorrow died Zheng side.

sentences.zh: 晉、楚、齊、衛聞之，皆曰：「非獨政能也，乃其姊亦烈女也。

translations.literal: When the people of Jin, Chu, Qi, and Wei heard of this, they all said: 'Not only was Nie Zheng extraordinary — his sister, too, was a woman of fierce conviction.'
translations.idiomatic: When word reached the people of Jin, Chu, Qi, and Wei, they all declared, "Not only was Nie Zheng remarkable — his sister was also a woman of fierce conviction."

sentences.zh: 鄉使政誠知其姊無濡忍之志，不重暴骸之難，必絕險千里以列其名，姊弟俱僇於韓市者，亦未必敢以身許嚴仲子也。

translations.literal: If Nie Zheng had truly known that his sister lacked the meek endurance to remain silent, that she would not shrink from the ordeal of his exposed corpse, but would travel a thousand li through peril to proclaim his name — so that brother and sister both met their end in the Han marketplace — he might never have dared pledge his life to Yan Zhongzi.
translations.idiomatic: "Had Nie Zheng known that his sister would refuse to bear her grief in silence — that she would brave a thousand li of peril to proclaim his name, so that brother and sister alike would meet their end in the marketplace of Han — he might never have dared pledge his life to Yan Zhongzi."

sentences.zh: 嚴仲子亦可謂知人能得士矣！」

translations.literal: Yan Zhongzi, too, may be said to have been a fine judge of character who knew how to win a man of resolve.
translations.idiomatic: "Yan Zhongzi may truly be called a man who understood others and knew how to win the loyalty of the resolute."

translations.literal: Jin, Chu, Qi, Wei heard, all said: 'Not only Zheng able, his sister also fierce woman. If made Zheng truly know his sister no soft endure will, not heavy expose corpse difficulty, must cut danger thousand li to establish his name, brother sister both executed Han market, also perhaps not dare with body promise Yan Zhongzi. Yan Zhongzi also can call know person able obtain scholars!'

sentences.zh: 其後二百二十餘年秦有荊軻之事。

translations.literal: More than two hundred and twenty years later, the affair of Jing Ke took place in Qin.
translations.idiomatic: More than two hundred and twenty years afterward, the affair of Jing Ke took place in Qin.

translations.literal: After two hundred twenty plus years Qin had Jing Ke affair.

sentences.zh: 荊軻者，衛人也。

translations.literal: Jing Ke was a man of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke was a native of the state of Wei.

sentences.zh: 其先乃齊人，徙於衛，衛人謂之慶卿。

translations.literal: His ancestors were originally from Qi and had relocated to Wei. The people of Wei called him Qing Qing.
translations.idiomatic: His forebears were originally from Qi but had migrated to Wei. The people of Wei knew him as Qing Qing.

sentences.zh: 而之燕，燕人謂之荊卿。

translations.literal: When he went to Yan, the people of Yan called him Jing Qing.
translations.idiomatic: When he traveled to Yan, the people of Yan called him Lord Jing.

translations.literal: Jing Ke was Wei person. His ancestor was Qi person, moved to Wei, Wei people called him Qing Qing. Went to Yan, Yan people called him Jing Qing.

sentences.zh: 荊卿好讀書擊劍，以術說衛元君，衛元君不用。

translations.literal: Lord Jing loved reading and swordsmanship, and with his arts he presented himself to Duke Yuan of Wei, but Duke Yuan did not employ him.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Jing was devoted to reading and swordsmanship. He presented his skills to Duke Yuan of Wei, but the duke declined to employ him.

sentences.zh: 其後秦伐魏，置東郡，徙衛元君之支屬於野王。

translations.literal: Later, Qin conquered Wei, established the Eastern Commandery, and relocated the branch relatives of Duke Yuan of Wei to Ye Wang.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, Qin conquered Wei and established the Eastern Commandery, relocating the collateral kinsmen of Duke Yuan of Wei to Ye Wang.

translations.literal: Jing Qing liked read books strike sword, with strategy persuaded Wei Yuan Jun, Wei Yuan Jun not used. Afterward Qin attacked Wei, established Dong commandery, moved Wei Yuan Jun branch relatives to Ye Wang.

sentences.zh: 荊軻嘗游過榆次，與蓋聶論劍，蓋聶怒而目之。

translations.literal: Jing Ke once traveled through Yuci, where he discussed swordsmanship with Gai Nie. Gai Nie glared at him angrily.
translations.idiomatic: On his travels, Jing Ke once passed through Yuci, where he discussed swordsmanship with the master swordsman Gai Nie. Gai Nie glared at him in displeasure.

sentences.zh: 荊軻出，人或言復召荊卿。

translations.literal: Jing Ke left. Someone suggested calling him back.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke departed. Someone suggested summoning him back.

sentences.zh: 蓋聶曰：「曩者吾與論劍有不稱者，吾目之；

translations.literal: Gai Nie said: 'Earlier when I discussed swords with him, there was something in his reasoning I found wrong, so I glared at him.'
translations.idiomatic: Gai Nie said, "When we discussed swordsmanship, he said something I found objectionable, so I glared at him."

sentences.zh: 試往，是宜去，不敢留。」

translations.literal: Try going to find him — he should have left already and will not dare remain.
translations.idiomatic: "Go check if you wish — he should already be gone. He would not dare remain."

sentences.zh: 使使往之主人，荊卿則已駕而去榆次矣。

translations.literal: A messenger went to his host's house, and indeed Jing Ke had already harnessed his horses and left Yuci.
translations.idiomatic: When a messenger reached his lodgings, Jing Ke had indeed already harnessed his horses and departed from Yuci.

sentences.zh: 使者還報，蓋聶曰：「固去也，吾曩者目攝之！」

translations.literal: The messenger returned and reported. Gai Nie said: 'Of course he has gone. My glare has already driven him away.'
translations.idiomatic: The messenger returned with the report. Gai Nie said, "Of course he has gone. My glare intimidated him into leaving."

translations.literal: Jing Ke once traveled passed Yu Ci, with Gai Nie discussed sword, Gai Nie angered glared at him. Jing Ke exited, person some said again summoned Jing Qing. Gai Nie said: 'Previously I with him discussed sword had disagreement, I glared at him; Try go, this suitable leave, not dare stay.' Made envoy go his lodging, Jing Qing then already harnessed departed Yu Ci. Envoy returned reported, Gai Nie said: 'Certainly left, my previously glared frightened him!'

sentences.zh: 荊軻游於邯鄲，魯句踐與荊軻博，爭道，魯句踐怒而叱之，荊軻嘿而逃去，遂不復會。

translations.literal: Jing Ke traveled to Handan, where he played a board game with Lu Goujian. They quarreled over a move, and Lu Goujian angrily cursed him. Jing Ke left in silence and never met him again.
translations.idiomatic: While traveling in Handan, Jing Ke played a board game with Lu Goujian. A dispute arose over a move, and Lu Goujian angrily berated him. Jing Ke withdrew in silence and never saw him again.

translations.literal: Jing Ke traveled Handan, Lu Gou Jian with Jing Ke gambled, contended road, Lu Gou Jian angered scolded him, Jing Ke silent escaped departed, thus not again met.

sentences.zh: 荊軻既至燕，愛燕之狗屠及善擊筑者高漸離。

translations.literal: After Jing Ke arrived in Yan, he grew fond of a dog butcher in Yan and of Gao Jianli, who was skilled at striking the zhu.
translations.idiomatic: After Jing Ke arrived in Yan, he became close to a certain dog butcher and to Gao Jianli, a man renowned for his skill with the zhu, a stringed instrument.

sentences.zh: 荊軻嗜酒，日與狗屠及高漸離飲於燕市，酒酣以往，高漸離擊筑，荊軻和而歌於市中，相樂也，已而相泣，旁若無人者。

translations.literal: Jing Ke loved wine and drank daily with the dog butcher and Gao Jianli in the marketplace of Yan. When the wine had flowed freely, Gao Jianli would strike the zhu and Jing Ke would sing along in the marketplace, delighting in each other, and then they would weep, as though there were no one else around.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke was fond of wine. Each day he drank with the dog butcher and Gao Jianli in the marketplace of Yan. When the wine flowed, Gao Jianli would play the zhu while Jing Ke sang along in the middle of the marketplace. They reveled in each other's company, and then wept together, as though no one else in the world existed.

sentences.zh: 荊軻雖游於酒人乎，然其為人沈深好書；

translations.literal: Although Jing Ke mingled with drunkards, his character was deep and contemplative, and he loved books.
translations.idiomatic: Though Jing Ke kept company with drinkers, his nature was in truth deep and contemplative, and he was devoted to books.

sentences.zh: 其所游諸侯，盡與其賢豪長者相結。

translations.literal: Wherever he traveled among the feudal lords, he formed bonds with all the worthy, heroic, and distinguished men.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he traveled among the states, he formed bonds with the most worthy, heroic, and distinguished men.

sentences.zh: 其之燕，燕之處士田光先生亦善待之，知其非庸人也。

translations.literal: When he came to Yan, the retired scholar Tian Guang also treated him well, knowing he was no ordinary man.
translations.idiomatic: When he arrived in Yan, the reclusive scholar Tian Guang treated him with great respect, recognizing that he was no ordinary man.

translations.literal: Jing Ke having reached Yan, loved Yan's dog butcher and good beat zhu Gao Jian Li. Jing Ke addicted wine, daily with dog butcher and Gao Jian Li drank Yan market, wine flowing then went, Gao Jian Li beat zhu, Jing Ke harmonized sang in market middle, mutually happy, already then mutually wept, side as no person. Jing Ke though wandered among wine people, yet his person deep profound good books; His traveled feudal lords, all befriended their worthy heroes elders. His went Yan, Yan's recluse Tian Guang mister also well treated him, knew he not common person.

sentences.zh: 居頃之，會燕太子丹質秦亡歸燕。

translations.literal: Before long, it happened that Crown Prince Dan of Yan, who had been a hostage in Qin, escaped and returned to Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Before long, Crown Prince Dan of Yan, who had been held as a hostage in Qin, made his escape and returned to Yan.

sentences.zh: 燕太子丹者，故嘗質於趙，而秦王政生於趙，其少時與丹驩。

translations.literal: Crown Prince Dan of Yan had formerly been a hostage in Zhao, and the King of Qin, Zheng, had been born in Zhao; in their youth, Dan and Zheng had been friends.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Dan had once been a hostage in Zhao, where the future King of Qin had been born. In their youth, the two had been friends.

sentences.zh: 及政立為秦王，而丹質於秦。

translations.literal: When Zheng became King of Qin, Dan was serving as a hostage in Qin.
translations.idiomatic: But when Zheng ascended the throne of Qin, Dan found himself a hostage in that very kingdom.

sentences.zh: 秦王之遇燕太子丹不善，故丹怨而亡歸。

translations.literal: The King of Qin treated Crown Prince Dan of Yan poorly, so Dan harbored resentment and fled home.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin treated Crown Prince Dan with contempt. Nursing a deep resentment, Dan fled back to Yan.

sentences.zh: 歸而求為報秦王者，國小，力不能。

translations.literal: Upon returning, he sought a way to take revenge against the King of Qin, but his state was small and its power insufficient.
translations.idiomatic: Once home, he sought a way to exact revenge upon the King of Qin, but his state was small and lacked the strength.

sentences.zh: 其後秦日出兵山東以伐齊、楚、三晉，稍蠶食諸侯，且至於燕，燕君臣皆恐禍之至。

translations.literal: Afterward, Qin sent armies day by day east of the mountains to attack Qi, Chu, and the Three Jin, gradually swallowing the feudal lords, until it drew near to Yan. The ruler and ministers of Yan all feared the catastrophe that was coming.
translations.idiomatic: In the years that followed, Qin relentlessly dispatched armies east of the mountains to attack Qi, Chu, and the Three Jin, devouring the feudal states one by one and drawing ever closer to Yan. The ruler and ministers of Yan trembled with dread at the approaching catastrophe.

sentences.zh: 太子丹患之，問其傅鞠武。

translations.literal: Crown Prince Dan worried about it and consulted his tutor Ju Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Dan was consumed with anxiety and consulted his tutor, Ju Wu.

sentences.zh: 武對曰：「秦地遍天下，威脅韓、魏、趙氏，北有甘泉、谷口之固，南有涇、渭之沃，擅巴、漢之饒，右隴、蜀之山，左關、殽之險，民眾而士厲，兵革有餘。

translations.literal: Wu replied: 'Qin's territory spans all under Heaven, its might threatens Han, Wei, and Zhao. To the north it has the strongholds of Ganquan and Gukou, to the south the fertile lands of the Jing and Wei rivers, it commands the riches of Ba and Han, to the right the mountains of Long and Shu, to the left the barriers of the Passes and Xiao. Its people are many, its warriors fierce, its arms and armor abundant.'
translations.idiomatic: Ju Wu replied, "Qin's territory spans the breadth of the realm. Its might menaces Han, Wei, and Zhao. To the north it holds the strongholds of Ganquan and Gukou; to the south, the fertile plains of the Jing and Wei rivers. It commands the riches of Ba and Han, the mountains of Long and Shu on its right, the barrier of the passes and Xiao on its left. Its people are numerous, its warriors battle-hardened, its arms and armor in surplus."

sentences.zh: 意有所出，則長城之南，易水以北，未有所定也。

translations.literal: Should it set its intent on conquest, then all south of the Long Wall and north of the Yi River would lie in the balance.
translations.idiomatic: "Should Qin turn its intentions toward us, then everything south of the Long Wall and north of the Yi River would hang in the balance."

sentences.zh: 柰何以見陵之怨，欲批其逆鱗哉！」

translations.literal: How can you, on account of a personal humiliation, seek to stroke the dragon's reverse scales?
translations.idiomatic: "How can you, out of a personal slight, presume to stroke the dragon's scales against the grain?"

sentences.zh: 丹曰：「然則何由？」

translations.literal: Dan said: 'Then what course is there?'
translations.idiomatic: Dan asked, "Then what course do we have?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「請入圖之。」

translations.literal: Wu replied: 'Allow me to retire and devise a plan.'
translations.idiomatic: Ju Wu replied, "Allow me to retire and give the matter thought."

translations.literal: Dwelt moment, met Yan crown prince Dan hostage Qin fled returned Yan. Yan crown prince Dan, formerly often hostage Zhao, Qin king Zheng born in Zhao, his young time with Dan friendly. When Zheng established as Qin king, Dan hostage in Qin. Qin king treated Yan crown prince Dan not good, thus Dan resented fled returned. Returned sought become avenge Qin king, state small, strength could not. Afterward Qin daily sent troops east to attack Qi, Chu, three Jin, gradually nibbled feudal lords, moreover reached Yan, Yan ruler ministers all feared calamity arrived. Crown prince Dan worried, asked his tutor Ju Wu. Wu responded: 'Qin land covers world, threatens Han, Wei, Zhao clans, north has Gan Quan, Gu Kou solid, south has Jing, Wei fertile, monopolizes Ba, Han rich, right Long, Shu mountains, left Guan, Xiao dangers, people many soldiers sharp, weapons armor surplus. Intent has out, then Great Wall south, Yi water north, not yet decided. How with seen humiliate resentment, wish slap his reverse scales!' Dan said: 'Then how?' Responded: 'Please let me enter and consider the maps.'

sentences.zh: 居有閒，秦將樊於期得罪於秦王，亡之燕，太子受而捨之。

translations.literal: After some time, the Qin general Fan Yuqi offended the King of Qin, fled to Yan, and the Crown Prince took him in and gave him shelter.
translations.idiomatic: After some time, the Qin general Fan Yuqi fell afoul of the King of Qin and fled to Yan, where Crown Prince Dan received him and gave him refuge.

sentences.zh: 鞠武諫曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Ju Wu remonstrated: 'This must not be done.'
translations.idiomatic: Ju Wu remonstrated, "This must not be done."

sentences.zh: 夫以秦王之暴而積怒於燕，足為寒心，又況聞樊將軍之所在乎？

translations.literal: Given the cruelty of the King of Qin and the fury he has accumulated against Yan, it is enough to make one shudder. How much more so when he learns of General Fan's whereabouts?
translations.idiomatic: "Given the cruelty of the King of Qin and the fury he has already directed at Yan, it is enough to make the blood run cold. How much worse when he learns where General Fan has taken refuge?"

sentences.zh: 是謂『委肉當餓虎之蹊』也，禍必不振矣！

translations.literal: This is what is called 'placing meat in the path of a hungry tiger' — disaster will be beyond remedy!
translations.idiomatic: "This is what they call 'placing meat in the path of a hungry tiger.' The disaster will be beyond all remedy!"

sentences.zh: 雖有管、晏，不能為之謀也。

translations.literal: Even with the wisdom of Guan Zhong and Yan Ying, they could not devise a plan for this.
translations.idiomatic: "Even Guan Zhong and Yan Ying at their wisest could not devise a way out of this."

sentences.zh: 願太子疾遣樊將軍入匈奴以滅口。

translations.literal: I beg the Crown Prince to send General Fan away to the Xiongnu at once, to remove the pretext.
translations.idiomatic: "I urge you to send General Fan away to the Xiongnu immediately, to remove all pretext for attack."

sentences.zh: 請西約三晉，南連齊、楚，北購於單于，其後乃可圖也。」

translations.literal: Then forge alliances with the Three Jin to the west, form ties with Qi and Chu to the south, and secure an arrangement with the Chanyu to the north — only afterward can we plan further.
translations.idiomatic: "Then ally with the Three Jin to the west, forge ties with Qi and Chu to the south, and reach an understanding with the Chanyu to the north. Only then can further plans be made."

sentences.zh: 太子曰：「太傅之計，曠日彌久，心惛然，恐不能須臾。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince said: 'The Grand Tutor's plan would take far too long. My heart is in turmoil, and I fear I cannot wait even a moment.'
translations.idiomatic: Crown Prince Dan said, "The Grand Tutor's plan would take far too long. My mind is in turmoil — I fear I cannot wait even a single day."

sentences.zh: 且非獨於此也，夫樊將軍窮困於天下，歸身於丹，丹終不以迫於彊秦而棄所哀憐之交，置之匈奴，是固丹命卒之時也。

translations.literal: And this is not the only consideration. General Fan, in his desperate hour, came to me for refuge. I would never, even under pressure from mighty Qin, abandon a man I have taken pity on and send him off to the Xiongnu. That would be the moment my fate is sealed.
translations.idiomatic: "And that is not all. General Fan came to me in his hour of desperation. I would never, no matter how great the threat from Qin, abandon a man I have shown compassion to and cast him out to the Xiongnu. That would be the moment I seal my own fate."

sentences.zh: 願太傅更慮之。」

translations.literal: I beg the Grand Tutor to reconsider.
translations.idiomatic: "I beg the Grand Tutor to reconsider."

sentences.zh: 鞠武曰：「夫行危欲求安，造禍而求福，計淺而怨深，連結一人之後交，不顧國家之大害，此所謂『資怨而助禍』矣。

translations.literal: Ju Wu said: 'To court danger and hope for safety, to invite disaster and seek blessings, to plan shallowly but harbor deep resentments, to bind yourself in friendship with a single man while disregarding great harm to the state — this is what is called fueling resentment and abetting disaster.'
translations.idiomatic: Ju Wu said, "To court danger in the hope of safety, to invite disaster in the pursuit of fortune, to scheme shallowly while nursing deep grievances, to forge a bond with one man while ignoring the peril to the entire state — this is what they call 'fueling resentment and abetting disaster.'"

sentences.zh: 夫以鴻毛燎於爐炭之上，必無事矣。

translations.literal: To place a swan's feather on a bed of hot coals — it will surely amount to nothing.
translations.idiomatic: "It is like laying a swan's feather upon a bed of hot coals — it will amount to nothing."

sentences.zh: 且以鵰鷙之秦，行怨暴之怒，豈足道哉！

translations.literal: Moreover, to face a Qin as fierce as a hawk and unleash its wrathful violence — is that even worth speaking of?
translations.idiomatic: "Moreover, to provoke a Qin as fierce as a raptor and invite its wrathful violence — is that even worth discussing?"

sentences.zh: 燕有田光先生，其為人智深而勇沈，可與謀。」

translations.literal: In Yan there is a man named Tian Guang, whose wisdom is deep and whose courage is resolute. You might consult him.
translations.idiomatic: "There is a man in Yan named Tian Guang, whose wisdom runs deep and whose courage is steadfast. You could consult with him."

sentences.zh: 太子曰：「願因太傅而得交於田先生，可乎？」

translations.literal: The Crown Prince said: 'I would like to make his acquaintance through the Grand Tutor. Would that be possible?'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince said, "I would like to be introduced to Master Tian through the Grand Tutor. Would that be possible?"

sentences.zh: 鞠武曰：「敬諾。」

translations.literal: Ju Wu said: 'I would be honored.'
translations.idiomatic: Ju Wu replied, "With the greatest pleasure."

sentences.zh: 出見田先生，道「太子願圖國事於先生也」。

translations.literal: He went out and met Tian Guang, telling him: 'The Crown Prince wishes to discuss affairs of state with you.'
translations.idiomatic: He went out to meet Tian Guang and conveyed the message: "The Crown Prince wishes to discuss affairs of state with you."

sentences.zh: 田光曰：「敬奉教。」

translations.literal: Tian Guang said: 'I respectfully accept.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guang replied, "I shall respectfully comply."

sentences.zh: 乃造焉。

translations.literal: And so he went to the prince's court.
translations.idiomatic: And so he proceeded to the prince's residence.

translations.literal: Dwelt some time, Qin general Fan Yuji offended Qin king, fled to Yan, crown prince received and sheltered him. Ju Wu advised: 'Cannot. With Qin king's violence and accumulated anger at Yan, enough to chill the heart, moreover if hears Fan general's location? This called 'placing meat on hungry tiger path', calamity certainly not recoverable! Though have Guan, Yan, cannot plan for it. Wish crown prince quickly send Fan general to Xiongnu to silence mouth. Please west ally three Jin, south connect Qi, Chu, north bribe Xiongnu chief, afterward then can plot.' Crown prince said: 'Grand Tutor's plan, broad days increasingly long, heart confused, fear cannot wait moment. Moreover not only this, Fan general poor distressed in world, gave body to Dan, Dan end not because pressed by strong Qin abandon pitied friendship, place in Xiongnu, this certainly Dan's doomed time. Wish Grand Tutor reconsider.' Ju Wu said: 'Go danger seek safety, create calamity seek fortune, plan shallow resentment deep, connect one person's later friendship, not consider state's great harm, this called 'provide resentment assist calamity'. With goose feather burn on furnace charcoal above, certainly no affair. Moreover with eagle owl Qin, execute resentful violent anger, how sufficient say!
