Yan has Tian Guang mister, his person wisdom deep courage profound, can with plan.' Crown prince said: 'Wish through Grand Tutor get befriend Tian mister, can?' Ju Wu said: 'Respectfully yes.' Exit saw Tian mister, said 'Crown prince wishes plot state affairs with mister.' Tian Guang said: 'Respectfully receive teaching.' Thus went to him.

sentences.zh: 太子逢迎，卻行為導，跪而蔽席。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince came out to greet him, walking backward to guide him, and knelt to brush the mat.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince came forth to greet him, walking backward as a mark of respect to guide his way, and knelt to clear the seat cushion for him.

sentences.zh: 田光坐定，左右無人，太子避席而請曰：「燕秦不兩立，願先生留意也。」

translations.literal: When Tian Guang was seated and there was no one nearby, the Crown Prince left his seat and made his request: 'Yan and Qin cannot both survive. I hope you will give this your attention.'
translations.idiomatic: Once Tian Guang was seated and they were alone, the Crown Prince left his seat and said earnestly, "Yan and Qin cannot both endure. I beg you to give this matter your deepest consideration."

sentences.zh: 田光曰：「臣聞騏驥盛壯之時，一日而馳千里；

translations.literal: Tian Guang said: 'I have heard that in the prime of a thoroughbred's strength, it can gallop a thousand li in a single day.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guang said, "I have heard that in its prime, a thoroughbred can gallop a thousand li in a single day."

sentences.zh: 至其衰老，駑馬先之。

translations.literal: But when it is old and spent, even a nag will outrun it.
translations.idiomatic: "But when it grows old and spent, even a common nag will outpace it."

sentences.zh: 今太子聞光盛壯之時，不知臣精已消亡矣。

translations.literal: Now the Crown Prince has heard of me in the days of my vigor, but does not know that my powers have already faded.
translations.idiomatic: "The Crown Prince has heard of me as I was in the days of my vigor, but does not realize that my strength has already faded."

sentences.zh: 雖然，光不敢以圖國事，所善荊卿可使也。」

translations.literal: Be that as it may, I dare not neglect affairs of state. The one I recommend to you — Lord Jing — could be sent on such a mission.
translations.idiomatic: "Be that as it may, I would not presume to neglect a matter of such importance. Allow me to recommend a man — Lord Jing — who could undertake such a mission."

sentences.zh: 太子曰：「願因先生得結交於荊卿，可乎？」

translations.literal: The Crown Prince said: 'I would like to make Lord Jing's acquaintance through you. Would that be possible?'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince said, "I would like to be introduced to Lord Jing through you. Would that be possible?"

sentences.zh: 田光曰：「敬諾。」

translations.literal: Tian Guang said: 'I would be honored.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guang replied, "With the greatest pleasure."

sentences.zh: 即起，趨出。

translations.literal: He immediately rose and hurried out.
translations.idiomatic: He rose at once and hurried out.

sentences.zh: 太子送至門，戒曰：「丹所報，先生所言者，國之大事也，願先生勿泄也！」

translations.literal: The Crown Prince escorted him to the gate and cautioned: 'What I have told you and what you have said concern a great affair of state. I beg you, sir, not to divulge it.'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince accompanied him to the gate and cautioned, "What I have confided in you and what you have spoken of today concern a great affair of state. I beg you, sir, do not let word of this escape."

sentences.zh: 田光俛而笑曰：「諾。」

translations.literal: Tian Guang bowed his head and smiled: 'I shall not.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guang bowed his head and smiled. "I shall not," he said.

sentences.zh: 僂行見荊卿，曰：「光與子相善，燕國莫不知。

translations.literal: He walked, stooped with age, to see Jing Ke and said: 'You and I are close, and all Yan knows it.'
translations.idiomatic: He made his way, stooped with age, to see Jing Ke and said, "You and I are close friends — all of Yan knows this."

sentences.zh: 今太子聞光壯盛之時，不知吾形已不逮也，幸而教之曰『燕秦不兩立，願先生留意也』。

translations.literal: Now the Crown Prince has heard of me in the days of my vigor, but does not know that my body can no longer keep up. He was kind enough to tell me that Yan and Qin cannot both survive, and asked me to give the matter my attention.
translations.idiomatic: "The Crown Prince heard of me as I was in my prime, but does not realize that my body can no longer keep pace. He was gracious enough to confide in me that Yan and Qin cannot both endure, and asked me to give the matter my attention."

sentences.zh: 光竊不自外，言足下於太子也，願足下過太子於宮。」

translations.literal: I did not presume to stand on ceremony, and I mentioned your name to the Crown Prince. I beg you to call upon the Crown Prince at his palace.
translations.idiomatic: "I did not presume to hold back, and I commended you to the Crown Prince. I beg you to call upon him at his palace."

sentences.zh: 荊軻曰：「謹奉教。」

translations.literal: Jing Ke said: 'I respectfully accept your instruction.'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke said, "I shall respectfully comply."

sentences.zh: 田光曰：「吾聞之，長者為行，不使人疑之。

translations.literal: Tian Guang said: 'I have heard it said that a man of principle, in the conduct of his affairs, must not let others suspect him.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian Guang said, "I have heard that a man of integrity, in his conduct, must give no one cause for suspicion."

sentences.zh: 今太子告光曰：『所言者，國之大事也，願先生勿泄』，是太子疑光也。

translations.literal: Now the Crown Prince has told me that our conversation concerns a great affair of state and has asked me not to divulge it. This means the Crown Prince suspects me.
translations.idiomatic: "Now the Crown Prince has cautioned me that our conversation touches upon a great affair of state and has asked me not to divulge it. This means the Crown Prince suspects me."

sentences.zh: 夫為行而使人疑之，非節俠也。」

translations.literal: For a man of honor to act in a way that invites suspicion — that is no conduct for a knight of integrity.
translations.idiomatic: "For a man to conduct himself in a way that invites suspicion — that is beneath a true man of honor."

sentences.zh: 欲自殺以激荊卿，曰：「願足下急過太子，言光已死，明不言也。」

translations.literal: Wishing to spur Jing Ke to action by his death, he said: 'I beg you to go at once to the Crown Prince and tell him that Tian Guang is dead, as proof that he did not speak.'
translations.idiomatic: Wishing to spur Jing Ke to action through his own death, he said, "I urge you to go to the Crown Prince at once and tell him that Tian Guang is dead — as proof that no word has escaped my lips."

sentences.zh: 因遂自刎而死。

translations.literal: Thereupon he cut his own throat and died.
translations.idiomatic: With that, he cut his own throat and died.

translations.literal: Crown prince welcomed, retreated walked guided, knelt spread mat. Tian Guang sat settled, left right no person, crown prince left seat requested: 'Yan Qin not two stand, wish mister attentive.' Tian Guang said: 'Minister heard thoroughbred in vigorous time, one day gallop thousand li; To its old age, nag precede it. Now crown prince hears my vigorous time, not know minister's essence already perished. Although so, Guang not dare with plot state affairs, what good Jing Qing can send.' Crown prince said: 'Wish through mister get befriend Jing Qing, can?' Tian Guang said: 'Respectfully yes.' Immediately rose, hurried out. Crown prince sent to door, warned: 'Dan what report, mister what speak, state's great affair, wish mister not leak!' Tian Guang bent laughed: 'Yes.' Bent walked saw Jing Ke, said: 'Guang with you friendly, Yan state none not know. Now crown prince hears Guang vigorous time, not know my form already not match, fortunately teach said 'Yan Qin not two stand, wish mister attentive'. Guang secretly not self-exclude, spoke you to crown prince, wish you visit crown prince at palace.' Jing Ke said: 'Respectfully receive teaching.' Tian Guang said: 'I heard, elder for conduct, not make people suspect. Now crown prince told Guang: 'What speak state's great affair, wish mister not leak', this crown prince suspects Guang. Do conduct yet make people suspect, not righteous knight.' Wished suicide to spur Jing Ke, said: 'Wish you quickly visit crown prince, speak Guang already dead, clarify not speak.' Thus then self-slit died.

sentences.zh: 荊軻遂見太子，言田光已死，致光之言。

translations.literal: Jing Ke then went to see the Crown Prince, told him that Tian Guang was dead, and conveyed his final words.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke went at once to the Crown Prince, told him of Tian Guang's death, and delivered his final message.

sentences.zh: 太子再拜而跪，膝行流涕，有頃而後言曰：「丹所以誡田先生毋言者，欲以成大事之謀也。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince bowed twice and knelt, then moved forward on his knees, weeping, and after a time he said: 'The reason I cautioned Master Tian not to speak was to ensure the success of a great plan.'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince bowed twice and dropped to his knees. He crawled forward, tears streaming down his face, and at last spoke: "The reason I cautioned Master Tian not to speak was to ensure the success of a great plan."

sentences.zh: 今田先生以死明不言，豈丹之心哉！」

translations.literal: Now Master Tian has died to prove he did not speak. Was that ever my intention?
translations.idiomatic: "Now Master Tian has died to prove his silence. Was this ever what I intended?"

sentences.zh: 荊軻坐定，太子避席頓首曰：「田先生不知丹之不肖，使得至前，敢有所道，此天之所以哀燕而不棄其孤也。

translations.literal: When Jing Ke was seated, the Crown Prince left his seat, bowed his head to the ground, and said: 'Master Tian, not knowing my unworthiness, has enabled you to come before me. That I may dare to speak of this plan is surely Heaven's way of pitying Yan and not abandoning its orphaned state.'
translations.idiomatic: Once Jing Ke was seated, the Crown Prince left his own seat, bowed his head to the floor, and said, "Master Tian, despite my unworthiness, enabled you to come before me so that I might dare to voice this plan. Surely this is Heaven's way of showing pity on Yan and not forsaking its orphaned people."

sentences.zh: 今秦有貪利之心，而欲不可足也。

translations.literal: Now Qin has a heart of insatiable greed, and its desires cannot be satisfied.
translations.idiomatic: "Now Qin is consumed by insatiable greed — its appetite knows no limit."

sentences.zh: 非盡天下之地，臣海內之王者，其意不厭。

translations.literal: Not until it has taken every inch of land under Heaven and made every king within the seas its vassal will its ambition be sated.
translations.idiomatic: "It will not rest until it has seized every inch of land under Heaven and reduced every king within the seas to vassalage."

sentences.zh: 今秦已虜韓王，盡納其地。

translations.literal: Now Qin has already captured the King of Han and annexed all his territory.
translations.idiomatic: "Qin has already captured the King of Han and annexed his entire realm."

sentences.zh: 又舉兵南伐楚，北臨趙；

translations.literal: It has also raised armies to attack Chu in the south and to menace Zhao in the north.
translations.idiomatic: "It has raised armies to attack Chu in the south and to menace Zhao in the north."

sentences.zh: 王翦將數十萬之眾距漳、鄴，而李信出太原、雲中。

translations.literal: Wang Jian leads hundreds of thousands of troops to the banks of the Zhang and toward Ye, while Li Xin advances from Taiyuan and Yunzhong.
translations.idiomatic: "Wang Jian commands hundreds of thousands of troops along the Zhang River and toward Ye, while Li Xin advances from Taiyuan and Yunzhong."

sentences.zh: 趙不能支秦，必入臣，入臣則禍至燕。

translations.literal: Zhao cannot withstand Qin and will inevitably submit. Once it submits, disaster will reach Yan.
translations.idiomatic: "Zhao cannot withstand Qin and will inevitably surrender. Once it falls, the disaster will reach Yan."

sentences.zh: 燕小弱，數困於兵，今計舉國不足以當秦。

translations.literal: Yan is small and weak, and has been exhausted by warfare. Even if we mobilized the entire state, it would not be enough to oppose Qin.
translations.idiomatic: "Yan is small and weak, exhausted by repeated wars. Even if we mobilized the entire state, it would not suffice to oppose Qin."

sentences.zh: 諸侯服秦，莫敢合從。

translations.literal: The feudal lords submit to Qin, and none dares join in an alliance.
translations.idiomatic: "The feudal lords have submitted to Qin, and not one dares to form an alliance against it."

sentences.zh: 丹之私計愚，以為誠得天下之勇士使於秦，闚以重利；

translations.literal: My private and foolish plan is this: if we could obtain the bravest warrior under Heaven and send him to Qin, to tempt the king with the promise of great gain —
translations.idiomatic: "In my private and admittedly naive estimation, if we could find the bravest warrior under Heaven and send him to Qin, dangling the promise of great gain before the king —"

sentences.zh: 秦王貪，其勢必得所願矣。

translations.literal: The King of Qin is greedy, and he would certainly agree to see him.
translations.idiomatic: "The King of Qin is greedy, and he would certainly grant the man an audience."

sentences.zh: 誠得劫秦王，使悉反諸侯侵地，若曹沫之與齊桓公，則大善矣；

translations.literal: If we could truly hold the King of Qin hostage and force him to return all the territories seized from the feudal lords, as Cao Mo did with Duke Huan of Qi, that would be the best outcome.
translations.idiomatic: "If we could hold the King of Qin hostage and force him to return all the territories seized from the feudal lords — as Cao Mo once did with Duke Huan of Qi — that would be the finest outcome."

sentences.zh: 則不可，因而刺殺之。

translations.literal: If that proves impossible, then we would assassinate him on the spot.
translations.idiomatic: "If that proves impossible, then we would assassinate him where he stands."

sentences.zh: 彼秦大將擅兵於外而內有亂，則君臣相疑，以其閒諸侯得合從，其破秦必矣。

translations.literal: With the great Qin generals commanding armies abroad and chaos within, the ruler and ministers would mistrust each other, and the feudal lords could seize the opportunity to form a united alliance. Then the destruction of Qin would be assured.
translations.idiomatic: "With Qin's great generals commanding armies far afield and turmoil erupting within, the ruler and his ministers would fall to mutual suspicion. The feudal lords could then seize the moment to form a united alliance, and the destruction of Qin would be assured."

sentences.zh: 此丹之上願，而不知所委命，唯荊卿留意焉。」

translations.literal: This is my highest wish, yet I do not know to whom to entrust this mission. I beg Lord Jing to give it his attention.
translations.idiomatic: "This is my highest hope, yet I do not know to whom I can entrust such a mission. I beg Lord Jing to give this matter his deepest attention."

sentences.zh: 久之，荊軻曰：「此國之大事也，臣駑下，恐不足任使。」

translations.literal: After a long silence, Jing Ke said: 'This is a great affair of state. I am dull and unworthy, and I fear I may not be equal to the task.'
translations.idiomatic: After a long silence, Jing Ke said, "This is a great affair of state. I am a man of limited ability, and I fear I am not equal to such a task."

sentences.zh: 太子前頓首，固請毋讓，然後許諾。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince bowed his head to the ground and earnestly implored him not to refuse, and at last Jing Ke consented.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince bowed low and earnestly implored him not to refuse. At last, Jing Ke accepted.

sentences.zh: 於是尊荊卿為上卿，舍上舍。

translations.literal: Thereupon Jing Ke was honored as a Senior Minister and lodged in the finest quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke was thereupon honored with the rank of Senior Minister and lodged in the finest quarters.

sentences.zh: 太子日造門下，供太牢具，異物閒進，車騎美女恣荊軻所欲，以順適其意。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince visited his door daily, providing feasts of the highest order, sending rare gifts at intervals, and furnishing him with carriages, horses, and beautiful women to gratify his every desire.
translations.idiomatic: Each day the Crown Prince called on him, providing the most lavish banquets, presenting rare gifts at intervals, and furnishing him with carriages, horses, and beautiful women to indulge his every desire.

translations.literal: Jing Ke thus saw crown prince, spoke Tian Guang already dead, conveyed Guang's words. Crown prince twice bowed knelt, knee walked flowing tears, had moment then spoke: 'Dan so warned Tian mister not speak, wished complete great affair plan. Now Tian mister with death clarify not speak, how Dan's heart!' Jing Ke sat settled, crown prince left seat bowed head: 'Tian mister not know Dan's unworthiness, made reach front, dared have way, this heaven so pity Yan not abandon its orphan. Now Qin has greedy profit heart, yet desire not satisfiable. Not exhaust world land, make sea inner king, his intent not sated. Now Qin already captured Han king, completely took its land. Again raised troops south attacked Chu, north faced Zhao; Wang Jian leads several ten thousand crowd resisted Zhang, Ye, while Li Xin exits Taiyuan, Yunzhong. Zhao cannot support Qin, must enter minister, enter minister then calamity reaches Yan.' Yan small weak, repeatedly distressed by troops, now plan whole state not sufficient to resist Qin. Feudal lords submit Qin, none dare vertical alliance. Dan's private plan foolish, considered truly get world's brave warriors send to Qin, spy with heavy profit; Qin king greedy, his situation must get what wish. Truly get kidnap Qin king, make completely return feudal lords invaded land, like Cao Mo with Qi Huan Gong, then very good; If cannot, moreover stab kill him. That Qin great generals monopolize troops outside while inner has chaos, then ruler ministers mutually suspect, with their interval feudal lords get vertical alliance, their break Qin certain. This Dan's upper wish, yet not know entrust life, only Jing Qing attentive.' Long time, Jing Ke said: 'This state's great affair, minister inferior, fear not sufficient undertake mission.' Crown prince front bowed head, firmly requested not decline, then permitted promised. Thus honored Jing Qing as upper minister, lodged upper lodge. Crown prince daily made door, supplied great feast prepared, rare objects frequent presented, chariot horses beauties indulged Jing Ke what desired, to comply suit his intent.

sentences.zh: 久之，荊軻未有行意。

translations.literal: After a long time, Jing Ke still showed no intention of setting out.
translations.idiomatic: Yet a long time passed and Jing Ke showed no sign of preparing to depart.

sentences.zh: 秦將王翦破趙，虜趙王，盡收入其地，進兵北略地至燕南界。

translations.literal: The Qin general Wang Jian had defeated Zhao, captured the King of Zhao, and annexed all his territory, then advanced his troops northward to the southern border of Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile, the Qin general Wang Jian crushed Zhao, captured its king, and annexed the entire kingdom. He then pushed his troops northward to the southern border of Yan.

sentences.zh: 太子丹恐懼，乃請荊軻曰：「秦兵旦暮渡易水，則雖欲長侍足下，豈可得哉！」

translations.literal: Crown Prince Dan, in terror, said to Jing Ke: 'The Qin army will cross the Yi River any day now. Though I wish to attend upon you at leisure, how can that be possible?'
translations.idiomatic: Terrified, Crown Prince Dan implored Jing Ke, "The Qin army could cross the Yi River any day now. Much as I would wish to have you here at leisure, how can that still be possible?"

sentences.zh: 荊軻曰：「微太子言，臣願謁之。

translations.literal: Jing Ke said: 'Even without the Crown Prince's words, I was about to request an audience on this matter.'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke said, "Even without Your Highness's words, I was about to raise this very matter."

sentences.zh: 今行而毋信，則秦未可親也。

translations.literal: But if I go now with nothing to win the King of Qin's trust, there will be no way to approach him.
translations.idiomatic: "But if I go with nothing to win the King of Qin's trust, there will be no way to get close to him."

sentences.zh: 夫樊將軍，秦王購之金千斤，邑萬家。

translations.literal: General Fan — the King of Qin has offered a thousand catties of gold and a fief of ten thousand households for his head.
translations.idiomatic: "General Fan — the King of Qin has placed a bounty of a thousand catties of gold and a fief of ten thousand households on his head."

sentences.zh: 誠得樊將軍首與燕督亢之地圖，奉獻秦王，秦王必說見臣，臣乃得有以報。」

translations.literal: If I could truly obtain General Fan's head and a map of the Dukang district of Yan, and present them to the King of Qin, the king would certainly be pleased to grant me an audience, and then I could fulfill the mission.
translations.idiomatic: "If I could obtain General Fan's head and a map of the Dukang district of Yan and present them to the King of Qin, the king would surely be delighted to grant me an audience. Then I would have my chance."

sentences.zh: 太子曰：「樊將軍窮困來歸丹，丹不忍以己之私而傷長者之意，願足下更慮之！」

translations.literal: The Crown Prince said: 'General Fan came to me in his hour of desperate need. I cannot bear to sacrifice him for my own private ends. I beg you to reconsider.'
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince said, "General Fan came to me in his darkest hour. I cannot bring myself to betray him for my own purposes. I beg you to find another way."

translations.literal: Long time, Jing Ke not had go intent. Qin general Wang Jian broke Zhao, captured Zhao king, completely took its land, advanced troops north seized land to Yan south border. Crown prince Dan feared, then requested Jing Ke: 'Qin troops morning evening cross Yi water, then though wish long serve you, how obtainable!' Jing Ke said: 'Without crown prince speak, minister wish see him. Now go yet no trust, then Qin not approachable. That Fan general, Qin king buys his head gold thousand jin, fief ten thousand households. Truly get Fan general head with Yan Du Kang land map, present Qin king, Qin king must pleased see minister, minister then can have to repay.' Crown prince said: 'Fan general poor distressed came submit Dan, Dan not bear with self private injure elder intention, wish you reconsider!'

sentences.zh: 荊軻知太子不忍，乃遂私見樊於期曰：「秦之遇將軍可謂深矣，父母宗族皆為戮沒。

translations.literal: Jing Ke knew that the Crown Prince could not bear to do it, so he went privately to see Fan Yuqi and said: 'Qin's treatment of the general has been cruel indeed — your father, mother, and entire clan have been put to death.'
translations.idiomatic: Knowing the Crown Prince could not bring himself to act, Jing Ke went privately to see Fan Yuqi and said, "Qin has treated you with the utmost cruelty — your parents and your entire clan have been slaughtered."

sentences.zh: 今聞購將軍首金千斤，邑萬家，將柰何？」

translations.literal: Now I hear a bounty of a thousand catties of gold and a fief of ten thousand households has been placed on your head. What will you do?
translations.idiomatic: "Now I hear that a bounty of a thousand catties of gold and a fief of ten thousand households has been placed on your head. What do you intend to do?"

sentences.zh: 於期仰天太息流涕曰：「於期每念之，常痛於骨髓，顧計不知所出耳！」

translations.literal: Fan Yuqi raised his face to Heaven, drew a deep breath, and wept: 'Whenever I think of it, the pain penetrates to the very marrow of my bones, but I see no way forward.'
translations.idiomatic: Fan Yuqi raised his eyes to Heaven, heaved a long sigh, and wept. "Whenever I think of it, the anguish cuts to the very marrow of my bones. Yet I see no way forward."

sentences.zh: 荊軻曰：「今有一言可以解燕國之患，報將軍之仇者，何如？」

translations.literal: Jing Ke said: 'Now I have a plan that could relieve the crisis of Yan and avenge the general's wrongs. What say you?'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke said, "I have a plan that could lift the peril from Yan and avenge your wrongs. What do you say?"

sentences.zh: 於期乃前曰：「為之柰何？」

translations.literal: Fan Yuqi stepped forward and said: 'How?'
translations.idiomatic: Fan Yuqi stepped forward eagerly. "How?"

sentences.zh: 荊軻曰：「願得將軍之首以獻秦王，秦王必喜而見臣，臣左手把其袖，右手揕其匈，然則將軍之仇報而燕見陵之愧除矣。

translations.literal: Jing Ke said: 'I wish to obtain the general's head to present to the King of Qin. The king will surely be delighted and grant me an audience. With my left hand I shall seize his sleeve, and with my right I shall plunge a dagger into his chest. Then the general's wrongs will be avenged and Yan's humiliation erased.'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke said, "I wish to present your head to the King of Qin. He will be overjoyed and grant me an audience. With my left hand I will seize his sleeve, and with my right I will plunge a dagger into his chest. The general's wrongs will be avenged, and Yan's humiliation will be wiped away."

sentences.zh: 將軍豈有意乎？」

translations.literal: Would the general be willing?
translations.idiomatic: "Would you be willing?"

sentences.zh: 樊於期偏袒搤捥而進曰：「此臣之日夜切齒腐心也，乃今得聞教！」

translations.literal: Fan Yuqi bared one shoulder, grasped his wrist, and stepped forward, saying: 'This is what I have ground my teeth and rotted my heart over, day and night — and now at last I hear the plan!'
translations.idiomatic: Fan Yuqi bared his shoulder, seized his own wrist, and declared, "This is what I have gnashed my teeth over and agonized about, day and night — and now at last I hear how it can be done!"

sentences.zh: 遂自剄。

translations.literal: And he cut his own throat.
translations.idiomatic: And he cut his own throat.

sentences.zh: 太子聞之，馳往，伏尸而哭，極哀。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince, hearing of it, rushed to the scene, threw himself upon the body, and wept with the deepest grief.
translations.idiomatic: When the Crown Prince heard the news, he raced to the scene, threw himself upon the body, and wept in the most wrenching grief.

sentences.zh: 既已不可柰何，乃遂盛樊於期首函封之。

translations.literal: Since nothing could be done to undo it, he placed Fan Yuqi's head in a box and sealed it.
translations.idiomatic: Since nothing could be undone, he placed Fan Yuqi's head in a casket and sealed it.

translations.literal: Jing Ke knew crown prince not bear, thus secretly saw Fan Yuji: 'Qin treated general can call deep, parents clan all executed exterminated. Now heard buy general head gold thousand jin, fief ten thousand households, will handle what?' Yuji faced heaven great sighed flowing tears: 'Yuji every think, often pained to bone marrow, but plan not know where out!' Jing Ke said: 'Now have one word can resolve Yan state suffering, repay general enmity, how?' Yuji then front: 'Do how?' Jing Ke said: 'Wish get general head to present Qin king, Qin king must pleased see minister, minister left hand grasp his sleeve, right hand stab his chest, then general enmity repaid Yan seen humiliate shame removed. General how intend?' Fan Yuji exposed shoulder choked cried advanced: 'This minister day night gnash teeth corrode heart, now get hear teaching!' Thus self-slit. Crown prince heard, galloped went, prostrated corpse cried, extreme sorrow. Already could not handle what, then placed Fan Yuji head in box sealed.

sentences.zh: 於是太子豫求天下之利匕首，得趙人徐夫人匕首，取之百金，使工以藥焠之，以試人，血濡縷，人無不立死者。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince then sought the sharpest dagger in the realm, obtained one made by a man from Zhao named Xu Furen for a hundred pieces of gold, and had a craftsman treat it with poison. When tested on a person, the blood soaked a thread and the victim died instantly.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince then sought the sharpest dagger in all the realm. He obtained one from a Zhao craftsman named Xu Furen, paying a hundred pieces of gold, and had an artisan temper it with poison. When tested, a single scratch drew blood that soaked through a thread of silk, and the victim died instantly.

sentences.zh: 乃裝為遣荊卿。

translations.literal: He then equipped Jing Ke for the mission.
translations.idiomatic: He then outfitted Jing Ke for the journey.

sentences.zh: 燕國有勇士秦舞陽，年十三，殺人，人不敢忤視。

translations.literal: In the state of Yan there was a brave warrior named Qin Wuyang, who had killed a man at the age of thirteen, and no one dared look him in the eye.
translations.idiomatic: In Yan there was a young warrior named Qin Wuyang who had killed a man at the age of thirteen. No one dared meet his gaze.

sentences.zh: 乃令秦舞陽為副。

translations.literal: He was ordered to serve as Jing Ke's assistant.
translations.idiomatic: He was assigned to serve as Jing Ke's assistant.

sentences.zh: 荊軻有所待，欲與俱；

translations.literal: Jing Ke was waiting for someone, wishing him to come along.
translations.idiomatic: But Jing Ke was waiting for a certain person, intending to bring him along.

sentences.zh: 其人居遠未來，而為治行。

translations.literal: That person lived far away and had not yet arrived, and Jing Ke was still making preparations for the journey.
translations.idiomatic: The man lived far away and had not yet arrived, and Jing Ke continued his preparations.

sentences.zh: 頃之，未發，太子遲之，疑其改悔，乃復請曰：「日已盡矣，荊卿豈有意哉？

translations.literal: After some time, when there was still no departure, the Crown Prince suspected he had changed his mind, and again requested: 'The days are running out. Does Lord Jing have any intention of going?'
translations.idiomatic: As time passed with still no departure, the Crown Prince grew suspicious that Jing Ke had changed his mind. He pressed him again: "The days are running out. Does Lord Jing still intend to go?"

sentences.zh: 丹請得先遣秦舞陽。」

translations.literal: I request permission to send Qin Wuyang ahead.
translations.idiomatic: "Allow me to send Qin Wuyang ahead."

sentences.zh: 荊軻怒，叱太子曰：「何太子之遣？

translations.literal: Jing Ke was furious and rebuked the Crown Prince: 'What is the Crown Prince sending him for?'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke was furious and snapped at the Crown Prince, "Why would Your Highness send him?"

sentences.zh: 往而不返者，豎子也！

translations.literal: To go and never return — that is the work of a mere boy!
translations.idiomatic: "To rush off on a one-way journey with no hope of return — that is the work of a reckless boy!"

sentences.zh: 且提一匕首入不測之彊秦，仆所以留者，待吾客與俱。

translations.literal: Moreover, to enter the unpredictable might of Qin with a single dagger — the reason I have delayed is that I am waiting for my companion to join me.
translations.idiomatic: "I am to enter the unfathomable power of Qin armed with nothing but a single dagger. The reason I have delayed is that I am waiting for my companion to arrive."

sentences.zh: 今太子遲之，請辭決矣！」

translations.literal: Now that the Crown Prince thinks me too slow, I shall take my leave!
translations.idiomatic: "Since the Crown Prince finds me too slow, then let me take my leave at once!"

sentences.zh: 遂發。

translations.literal: And so he departed.
translations.idiomatic: And so he set forth.

translations.literal: Thus crown prince beforehand sought world sharp dagger, got Zhao person Xu lady dagger, bought hundred jin gold, made artisan with medicine temper, tested on person, blood wet thread, person none not immediately died. Then prepared send Jing Qing. Yan state had brave warrior Qin Wuyang, age thirteen, killed person, people dared not offend look. Thus commanded Qin Wuyang as deputy. Jing Ke had waited person, wished with go; That person dwelt far not come, yet prepared journey. Moment, not issued, crown prince delayed him, suspected his change regret, then requested: 'Day already exhausted, Jing Qing how intend? Dan please get first send Qin Wuyang.' Jing Ke angered, scolded crown prince: 'What crown prince send? Go not return, boy child! Moreover carry one dagger enter immeasurable strong Qin, servant so stay, wait my guest with go. Now crown prince delays, please leave decided!' Thus issued.

sentences.zh: 太子及賓客知其事者，皆白衣冠以送之。

translations.literal: The Crown Prince and all the guests who knew of the affair saw him off, dressed in white caps and robes of mourning.
translations.idiomatic: The Crown Prince and all the guests who knew of the mission came to see him off, dressed in the white caps and robes of mourning.

sentences.zh: 至易水之上，既祖，取道，高漸離擊筑，荊軻和而歌，為變徵之聲，士皆垂淚涕泣。

translations.literal: At the bank of the Yi River, after the farewell sacrifice and before taking to the road, Gao Jianli struck the zhu, and Jing Ke sang in harmony, in the key of the mournful zhi mode. The warriors all wept.
translations.idiomatic: At the bank of the Yi River, after the farewell sacrifice and before setting out on the road, Gao Jianli struck the zhu and Jing Ke sang in harmony, his voice in the mournful zhi mode. Every warrior wept.

sentences.zh: 又前而為歌曰：「風蕭蕭兮易水寒，壯士一去兮不復還！」

translations.literal: He stepped forward again and sang: 'The wind moans and the Yi River is cold; once the brave man goes, he shall never return!'
translations.idiomatic: He stepped forward and sang once more: "The wind howls and the Yi River runs cold — the brave man departs, never to return!"

sentences.zh: 復為羽聲慨，士皆瞋目，發盡上指冠。

translations.literal: Then he shifted to the heroic yu mode. The warriors all glared, their hair bristling until their caps were lifted.
translations.idiomatic: Then he shifted to the stirring yu mode. Every warrior's eyes blazed, and their hair stood on end until it lifted their caps.

sentences.zh: 於是荊軻就車而去，終已不顧。

translations.literal: Thereupon Jing Ke mounted his chariot and rode away, and to the very end he never looked back.
translations.idiomatic: With that, Jing Ke mounted his chariot and departed. He never once looked back.

translations.literal: Crown prince and guests knew affair, all white clothes hats to send him. Reached Yi water above, already sacrificed, took road, Gao Jianli beat zhu, Jing Ke harmonized sang, made change zhi sound, scholars all hung tears wept. Again front made song: 'Wind soughs Yi water cold, brave man one go not return!' Again made yu sound lofty, scholars all stared eyes, hair all up pointed caps. Thus Jing Ke approached carriage departed, end not look back.

sentences.zh: 遂至秦，持千金之資幣物，厚遺秦王寵臣中庶子蒙嘉。

translations.literal: He arrived in Qin and, carrying gifts worth a thousand pieces of gold, generously bribed Meng Jia, a favorite minister of the King of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: He arrived in Qin bearing gifts worth a thousand pieces of gold, and lavished them upon Meng Jia, a trusted minister of the King of Qin.

sentences.zh: 嘉為先言於秦王曰：「燕王誠振怖大王之威，不敢舉兵以逆軍吏，願舉國為內臣，比諸侯之列，給貢職如郡縣，而得奉守先王之宗廟。

translations.literal: Meng Jia spoke first to the King of Qin on his behalf: 'The King of Yan truly trembles before the great might of Your Majesty. He dares not raise arms against your forces, and begs to submit his entire state as a vassal, to be ranked among the feudal lords and to pay tribute like a commandery, so that he may continue the sacrifices at his ancestral temple.'
translations.idiomatic: Meng Jia spoke to the King of Qin on his behalf: "The King of Yan truly trembles before Your Majesty's might. He dares not raise arms against your forces and begs to submit his entire state as your vassal, to take his place among the feudal lords and render tribute as would a commandery or county, that he may preserve the sacrifices at his ancestral temple."

sentences.zh: 恐懼不敢自陳，謹斬樊於期之頭，及獻燕督亢之地圖，函封，燕王拜送于庭，使使以聞大王，唯大王命之。」

translations.literal: In fear and trepidation he dares not present himself, but has reverently beheaded Fan Yuqi and offers a map of the Dukang district of Yan, sealed in a box. The King of Yan bowed in farewell at his court and dispatched a messenger to report to Your Majesty. He awaits Your Majesty's command.
translations.idiomatic: "In his fear he dares not present himself in person. He has reverently beheaded Fan Yuqi and offers a map of the Dukang district of Yan, both sealed in caskets. The King of Yan bowed in farewell at his own court and dispatched an envoy to report to Your Majesty. He awaits Your Majesty's command."

sentences.zh: 秦王聞之，大喜，乃朝服，設九賓，見燕使者咸陽宮。

translations.literal: When the King of Qin heard this, he was overjoyed. He donned his court robes, arranged the full nine-guest ceremony, and received the Yan envoy at Xianyang Palace.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Qin heard this, he was overjoyed. He donned his formal court robes, arranged the full nine-rank ceremonial reception, and received the envoy from Yan at Xianyang Palace.

sentences.zh: 荊軻奉樊於期頭函，而秦舞陽奉地圖柙，以次進。

translations.literal: Jing Ke held the casket containing Fan Yuqi's head, while Qin Wuyang held the map case, and they advanced in order.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke carried the casket containing Fan Yuqi's head, while Qin Wuyang carried the map case. They advanced in order toward the throne.

sentences.zh: 至陛，秦舞陽色變振恐，群臣怪之。

translations.literal: When they reached the steps of the throne, Qin Wuyang's face changed color and he shook with fear. The assembled ministers looked at him with suspicion.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached the steps leading to the throne, Qin Wuyang's face went pale and he began to tremble with fear. The assembled courtiers eyed him with suspicion.

sentences.zh: 荊軻顧笑舞陽，前謝曰：「北蕃蠻夷之鄙人，未嘗見天子，故振慴。

translations.literal: Jing Ke looked back at Wuyang and smiled, then stepped forward to apologize: 'He is a rude barbarian from the northern frontier who has never been before the Son of Heaven, and so he trembles with awe.'
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke glanced back at Wuyang and smiled, then stepped forward to apologize: "He is but a rough man from the northern frontier who has never stood before the Son of Heaven. Naturally, he trembles with awe."

sentences.zh: 願大王少假借之，使得畢使於前。」

translations.literal: I beg Your Majesty to overlook his behavior and allow him to complete his mission before you.
translations.idiomatic: "I beg Your Majesty to overlook his behavior and allow him to complete his duty before you."

sentences.zh: 秦王謂軻曰：「取舞陽所持地圖。」

translations.literal: The King of Qin said to Jing Ke: 'Bring me the map that Wuyang holds.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin said to Jing Ke, "Bring me the map that Wuyang carries."

sentences.zh: 軻既取圖奏之，秦王發圖，圖窮而匕首見。

translations.literal: Jing Ke took the map and presented it. When the King unrolled the map, the dagger was revealed at the end.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke took the map and presented it to the king. As the king unrolled it, the dagger was revealed at the very end.

sentences.zh: 因左手把秦王之袖，而右手持匕首揕之。

translations.literal: With his left hand he seized the King of Qin's sleeve, and with his right hand he grasped the dagger and thrust at him.
translations.idiomatic: In one motion Jing Ke seized the King of Qin's sleeve with his left hand and snatched up the dagger with his right, thrusting at the king.

sentences.zh: 未至身，秦王驚，自引而起，袖絕。

translations.literal: Before the blade reached the king's body, the King of Qin recoiled in alarm, springing to his feet, and his sleeve tore free.
translations.idiomatic: But before the blade reached his body, the King of Qin recoiled in alarm and sprang to his feet. His sleeve tore free.

sentences.zh: 拔劍，劍長，操其室。

translations.literal: He reached for his sword, but the blade was long and caught in its scabbard.
translations.idiomatic: He reached for his sword, but the blade was too long and caught in its scabbard.

sentences.zh: 時惶急，劍堅，故不可立拔。

translations.literal: In his panic and haste, the sword was stuck fast and could not be drawn.
translations.idiomatic: In his panic, the sword jammed fast and would not come free.

sentences.zh: 荊軻逐秦王，秦王環柱而走。

translations.literal: Jing Ke chased the King of Qin, and the king ran around a pillar.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Ke pursued the King of Qin, and the king fled around a bronze pillar.

sentences.zh: 群臣皆愕，卒起不意，盡失其度。

translations.literal: The ministers were all stunned. The crisis arose so suddenly and unexpectedly that they all lost their composure.
translations.idiomatic: The assembled ministers were stupefied. The crisis erupted so suddenly that every one of them lost his composure.

sentences.zh: 而秦法，群臣侍殿上者不得持尺寸之兵；

translations.literal: By Qin law, ministers attending in the throne hall were not permitted to carry even the smallest weapon.
translations.idiomatic: By the law of Qin, ministers attending in the throne hall were forbidden to carry even the smallest weapon.

sentences.zh: 諸郎中執兵皆陳殿下，非有詔召不得上。

translations.literal: The armed palace guards were all stationed below the hall and could not ascend without a royal summons.
translations.idiomatic: The armed palace guards were all stationed below the hall and could not ascend without a direct royal command.

sentences.zh: 方急時，不及召下兵，以故荊軻乃逐秦王。

translations.literal: In the urgency of the moment, there was no time to summon the guards below, and so Jing Ke continued to chase the King of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: In the urgency of the moment, there was no time to summon the guards from below. And so Jing Ke continued to chase the King of Qin.

sentences.zh: 而卒惶急，無以擊軻，而以手共搏之。

translations.literal: In the panic and confusion, there was nothing to strike Jing Ke with, so the ministers struck at him with their bare hands.
translations.idiomatic: In the chaos and confusion, with nothing to strike Jing Ke with, the ministers resorted to grappling at him with their bare hands.

sentences.zh: 是時侍醫夏無且以其所奉藥囊提荊軻也。

translations.literal: At that moment, the court physician Xia Wuju hurled his medicine bag at Jing Ke.
translations.idiomatic: At that moment, the court physician Xia Wuju hurled his medicine bag at Jing Ke.

sentences.zh: 秦王方環柱走，卒惶急，不知所為，左右乃曰：「王負劍！」

translations.literal: The King of Qin was still running around the pillar, in such panic and confusion that he did not know what to do, when those around him cried out: 'Sling your sword over your back, Your Majesty!'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin was still running around the pillar in blind panic, unable to think clearly, when his attendants shouted, "Sling the sword across your back, Your Majesty!"

sentences.zh: 負劍，遂拔以擊荊軻，斷其左股。

translations.literal: He slung the sword over his back, drew it, and struck Jing Ke, severing his left thigh.
translations.idiomatic: He swung the sword across his back, managed to draw it at last, and struck Jing Ke, severing his left thigh.

sentences.zh: 荊軻廢，乃引其匕首以擿秦王，不中，中桐柱。

translations.literal: Jing Ke, crippled, hurled his dagger at the King of Qin but missed, striking a bronze pillar instead.
translations.idiomatic: Crippled, Jing Ke hurled the dagger at the King of Qin. It missed, striking a bronze pillar instead.

sentences.zh: 秦王復擊軻，軻被八創。

translations.literal: The King of Qin struck Jing Ke again, inflicting eight wounds.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qin struck again, inflicting eight wounds on Jing Ke.

sentences.zh: 軻自知事不就，倚柱而笑，箕踞以罵曰：「事所以不成者，以欲生劫之，必得約契以報太子也。」

translations.literal: Jing Ke, knowing his mission had failed, leaned against a pillar and laughed, then sat with legs spread wide and cursed: 'The reason the mission failed is that I wanted to seize you alive and force you to sign a covenant to repay the Crown Prince.'
translations.idiomatic: Knowing his mission had failed, Jing Ke slumped against a pillar and laughed. He spread his legs wide and spat, "The reason I failed is that I wanted to take you alive — to force you to sign a covenant and repay the Crown Prince."

sentences.zh: 於是左右既前殺軻，秦王不怡者良久。

translations.literal: Thereupon the attendants rushed forward and killed Jing Ke. The King of Qin remained uneasy for a long time afterward.
translations.idiomatic: The attendants rushed forward and killed Jing Ke. The King of Qin remained shaken for a long time afterward.

sentences.zh: 已而論功，賞群臣及當坐者各有差，而賜夏無且黃金二百溢，曰：「無且愛我，乃以藥囊提荊軻也。」

translations.literal: When merits were assessed and rewards distributed, each minister and those who bore blame received their due. Xia Wuju was given two hundred measures of gold, with the remark: 'Wuju cared for me, for it was he who threw his medicine bag at Jing Ke.'
translations.idiomatic: When merits were assessed and rewards dispensed — each minister and those who bore fault receiving his due — Xia Wuju was given two hundred measures of gold. The king declared, "Wuju was devoted to me — it was he who threw his medicine bag at Jing Ke."

translations.literal: Thus reached Qin, held thousand jin gift currency objects, thick presented Qin king favored minister middle minister Zi Meng Jia. Jia for first spoke Qin king: 'Yan king truly shaken terrified great king's might, not dare raise troops to oppose army officials, wish whole state become inner minister, compare feudal lords ranks, give tribute duty like commandery county, able maintain guard former king ancestral temple. Fear not dare self-state, respectfully behead Fan Yuji head, and offer Yan Du Kang land map, box sealed, Yan king bowed sent at court, made envoy to hear great king, only great king command.' Qin king heard, greatly pleased, court robes, set nine guests, saw Yan envoy Xianyang palace. Jing Ke presented Fan Yuji head box, Qin Wuyang presented land map case, by order advanced. Reached stairs, Qin Wuyang color changed trembled feared, ministers strange it. Jing Ke looked back laughed Wuyang, front apologized: 'North barbarian uncivilized rustic person, not tasted see emperor, thus tremble frightened. Wish great king little lenient, make him complete mission at front.' Qin king told Ke: 'Take Wuyang held land map.' Ke having taken map presented, Qin king unrolled map, map exhausted dagger appeared. Thus left hand grasped Qin king sleeve, right hand held dagger stabbed him. Not reached body, Qin king startled, self pulled rose, sleeve broke. Drew sword, sword long, gripped its scabbard. Time panicked urgent, sword firm, thus not could immediately draw. Jing Ke pursued Qin king, Qin king circled pillar walked. Ministers all astonished, suddenly rose unexpected, all lost their measure. Qin law, ministers served hall above not permitted hold inch weapons; Various langzhong held weapons all arrayed hall below, not have decree summon not could ascend. When urgent time, not reached summon below troops, thus Jing Ke pursued Qin king. Yet finally panicked urgent, no with strike Ke, with hands together fought him. At time attending physician Xia Wuqian with his carried medicine bag struck Jing Ke. Qin king just circled pillar walked, finally panicked urgent, not knew what do, left right then said: 'King carry sword!' Carry sword, then drew to strike Jing Ke, severed his left thigh. Jing Ke crippled, then drew his dagger to throw Qin king, not hit, hit paulownia pillar. Qin king again struck Ke, Ke suffered eight wounds. Ke self knew affair not succeed, leaned pillar laughed, sat cross-legged cursed: 'Affair so not complete, because wished alive kidnap him, must get treaty to repay crown prince.' Thus left right having front killed Ke, Qin king not pleased long time. Already then discussed merit, rewarded ministers and should sit each different, granted Xia Wuqian yellow gold two hundred yi, said: 'Wuqian loved me, thus with medicine bag struck Jing Ke.'

sentences.zh: 於是秦王大怒，益發兵詣趙，詔王翦軍以伐燕。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Qin was greatly enraged. He sent additional troops against Zhao and ordered Wang Jian's army to attack Yan.
translations.idiomatic: After this, the King of Qin was consumed with fury. He dispatched additional troops against Zhao and ordered Wang Jian's army to attack Yan.

sentences.zh: 十月而拔薊城。

translations.literal: In the tenth month they took the capital Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Within ten months they captured the capital, Ji.

sentences.zh: 燕王喜、太子丹等盡率其精兵東保於遼東。

translations.literal: King Xi of Yan, Crown Prince Dan, and the rest led their best troops east and took refuge in Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: King Xi of Yan, Crown Prince Dan, and the rest retreated eastward with their finest troops and took refuge in Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 秦將李信追擊燕王急，代王嘉乃遺燕王喜書曰：「秦所以尤追燕急者，以太子丹故也。

translations.literal: The Qin general Li Xin pressed the pursuit of the King of Yan relentlessly. King Jia of Dai sent a letter to King Xi saying: 'The reason Qin is pursuing Yan so urgently is because of Crown Prince Dan.'
translations.idiomatic: The Qin general Li Xin pressed the pursuit of the King of Yan relentlessly. King Jia of Dai sent a letter to King Xi: "The reason Qin pursues Yan so urgently is Crown Prince Dan."

sentences.zh: 今王誠殺丹獻之秦王，秦王必解，而社稷幸得血食。」

translations.literal: If Your Majesty would truly kill Dan and present his head to the King of Qin, the King of Qin would surely relent, and the altars of your state might yet survive.
translations.idiomatic: "If Your Majesty would kill Dan and present his head to the King of Qin, the king would surely relent, and the altars of your state might yet be preserved."

sentences.zh: 其後李信追丹，丹匿衍水中，燕王乃使使斬太子丹，欲獻之秦。

translations.literal: Afterward, Li Xin pursued Dan, and Dan hid along the Yan River. The King of Yan then sent men to behead Crown Prince Dan, hoping to present his head to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, Li Xin pursued Crown Prince Dan, who went into hiding along the Yan River. The King of Yan then sent men to behead his own son, intending to present the head to Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦復進兵攻之。

translations.literal: Qin continued to advance and attack.
translations.idiomatic: But Qin pressed its attack regardless.

sentences.zh: 後五年，秦卒滅燕，虜燕王喜。

translations.literal: Five years later, Qin finally destroyed Yan and captured King Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later, Qin at last destroyed Yan and captured King Xi.

translations.literal: Thus Qin king greatly angered, increased sent troops to Zhao, commanded Wang Jian army to attack Yan. Tenth month then captured Ji city. Yan king Xi, crown prince Dan etc. completely led their elite troops east defended at Liaodong. Qin general Li Xin pursued Yan king urgent, Dai king Jia then sent Yan king Xi letter: 'Qin so especially pursued Yan urgent, because crown prince Dan. Now king truly kill Dan offer Qin king, Qin king must relieve, state altars fortunately get blood food.' Afterward Li Xin pursued Dan, Dan hid Yan water, Yan king then made envoy behead crown prince Dan, wished offer Qin. Qin again advanced troops attacked. After five years, Qin finally extinguished Yan, captured Yan king Xi.

sentences.zh: 其明年，秦并天下，立號為皇帝。

translations.literal: The following year, Qin unified all under Heaven and assumed the title of Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, Qin unified all under Heaven and assumed the title of Emperor.

sentences.zh: 於是秦逐太子丹、荊軻之客，皆亡。

translations.literal: Thereupon Qin hunted down the associates of Crown Prince Dan and Jing Ke, and they all fled.
translations.idiomatic: Qin then hunted down every associate of Crown Prince Dan and Jing Ke. All of them fled.

sentences.zh: 高漸離變名姓為人庸保，匿作於宋子。

translations.literal: Gao Jianli changed his name and went into hiding as a hired laborer in the town of Songzi.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Jianli changed his name and went into hiding as a hired laborer in the town of Songzi.

sentences.zh: 久之，作苦，聞其家堂上客擊筑，傍偟不能去。

translations.literal: After a long time, worn down by hard labor, he heard the sound of a guest playing the zhu in the main hall of his master's house, and lingered, unable to leave.
translations.idiomatic: After a long time, worn down by hard labor, he overheard a guest playing the zhu in the main hall of his employer's house. He lingered outside, unable to tear himself away.

sentences.zh: 每出言曰：「彼有善有不善。」

translations.literal: Each time he would remark: 'That playing has merits and flaws.'
translations.idiomatic: Each time, he would comment, "That playing has its merits and its flaws."

sentences.zh: 從者以告其主，曰：「彼庸乃知音，竊言是非。」

translations.literal: A fellow servant reported this to the master, saying: 'That hired man seems to know music — he keeps commenting on the playing.'
translations.idiomatic: A fellow servant reported this to the master: "That hired man seems to know music — he keeps offering his opinions on the playing."

sentences.zh: 家丈人召使前擊筑，一坐稱善，賜酒。

translations.literal: The head of the household summoned him and had him play the zhu before the assembled guests. Everyone praised his skill, and he was given wine.
translations.idiomatic: The head of the household summoned him and had him play the zhu before the assembled guests. Everyone praised his skill, and he was rewarded with wine.

sentences.zh: 而高漸離念久隱畏約無窮時，乃退，出其裝匣中筑與其善衣，更容貌而前。

translations.literal: Gao Jianli, having spent so long in concealment, fearing and constrained endlessly, withdrew, took out his own fine zhu and good clothes from their case, changed his appearance, and came forward again.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Jianli, weary of living in fear and concealment without end, withdrew and took from his traveling case his own fine zhu and good clothes. He changed his appearance and came forward once more.

sentences.zh: 舉坐客皆驚，下與抗禮，以為上客。

translations.literal: All the seated guests were astonished. They descended to meet him with full courtesies and treated him as the guest of honor.
translations.idiomatic: Every guest in the hall was astonished. They rose to greet him with full ceremonial courtesy and seated him in the place of honor.

sentences.zh: 使擊筑而歌，客無不流涕而去者。

translations.literal: When he played the zhu and sang, not a single guest left without tears.
translations.idiomatic: When he played the zhu and sang, not a single guest departed without tears in his eyes.

sentences.zh: 宋子傳客之，聞於秦始皇。

translations.literal: The people of Songzi passed him from house to house as a guest, and his fame reached the First Emperor of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Songzi hosted him from house to house, and his fame eventually reached the ears of the First Emperor of Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦始皇召見，人有識者，乃曰：「高漸離也。」

translations.literal: The First Emperor of Qin summoned him for an audience. Someone present recognized him and said: 'It is Gao Jianli.'
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor summoned him for an audience. Someone in the court recognized him and said, "That is Gao Jianli."

sentences.zh: 秦皇帝惜其善擊筑，重赦之，乃矐其目。

translations.literal: The Emperor, valuing his skill with the zhu, spared his life but had his eyes put out.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor, loath to waste such a musician, spared his life but had his eyes put out.

sentences.zh: 使擊筑，未嘗不稱善。

translations.literal: He was made to play the zhu, and his playing was always praised.
translations.idiomatic: He was made to play the zhu at court, and his playing never failed to win praise.

sentences.zh: 稍益近之，高漸離乃以鉛置筑中，復進得近，舉筑樸秦皇帝，不中。

translations.literal: Gradually he was allowed closer. Gao Jianli then filled his zhu with lead, and when next brought close, he swung the zhu at the Emperor but missed.
translations.idiomatic: Gradually he was allowed closer and closer. Gao Jianli then filled his zhu with lead. When he was next brought near, he swung the weighted instrument at the Emperor — but missed.

sentences.zh: 於是遂誅高漸離，終身不復近諸侯之人。

translations.literal: Thereupon Gao Jianli was executed. For the rest of his life, the Emperor never again allowed anyone from the feudal states to come near him.
translations.idiomatic: Gao Jianli was executed on the spot. For the rest of his life, the Emperor never again allowed anyone from the former feudal states to approach him.

translations.literal: Its next year, Qin united world, established title emperor. Thus Qin pursued crown prince Dan, Jing Ke guests, all fled. Gao Jianli changed name surname became person menial servant, hid worked at Song Zi. Long time, work bitter, heard his family hall guest beat zhu, eavesdropped could not leave. Every exit spoke: 'That has good has not good.' Followers told his master, said: 'That menial actually knows sound, secretly speaks right wrong.' Family elder summoned made front beat zhu, one seat praised good, granted wine. Gao Jianli thought long hidden feared restraint no end time, then retreated, exited his outfit box zhu with his good clothes, changed appearance front. Whole seat guests all startled, descended with equal ritual, considered upper guest. Made beat zhu sang, guests none not flowing tears departed. Song Zi transmitted guests, heard by Qin Shi Huang. Qin Shi Huang summoned saw, person had recognized, then said: 'Gao Jianli.' Qin emperor cherished his good beat zhu, heavy pardoned him, then blinded his eyes. Made beat zhu, not tasted not praised good. Gradually increased close him, Gao Jianli then with lead placed zhu middle, again advanced got close, raised zhu struck Qin emperor, not hit. Thus then executed Gao Jianli, lifetime not again approached feudal lord persons.

sentences.zh: 魯句踐已聞荊軻之刺秦王，私曰：「嗟乎，惜哉其不講於刺劍之術也！

translations.literal: Lu Goujian, when he heard that Jing Ke had tried to assassinate the King of Qin, said to himself: 'Alas! What a pity that he did not apply himself to the art of the sword!'
translations.idiomatic: When Lu Goujian learned of Jing Ke's attempt on the King of Qin, he said to himself, "Alas! What a pity he never mastered the art of the blade!"

sentences.zh: 甚矣吾不知人也！

translations.literal: How greatly I misjudged the man!
translations.idiomatic: "How gravely I misjudged the man!"

sentences.zh: 曩者吾叱之，彼乃以我為非人也！」

translations.literal: When I cursed him in the past, he must have thought me unworthy of his notice!
translations.idiomatic: "When I berated him that day, he must have thought me beneath his notice!"

translations.literal: Lu Goujian already heard Jing Ke stabbed Qin king, privately said: 'Alas, pity his not practiced sword stabbing technique! Extreme my not know person! Previously I scolded him, he then considered me not person!'

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：世言荊軻，其稱太子丹之命，「天雨粟，馬生角」也，太過。

translations.literal: The Grand Historian remarks: The world speaks of Jing Ke, and they cite Crown Prince Dan's tale of 'grain raining from the sky and horses growing horns' — that is going too far.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: The world speaks of Jing Ke, and people cite the tale that Crown Prince Dan commanded grain to rain from the sky and horses to sprout horns. That is a great exaggeration.

sentences.zh: 又言荊軻傷秦王，皆非也。

translations.literal: Others say Jing Ke wounded the King of Qin, which is also false.
translations.idiomatic: Others claim that Jing Ke wounded the King of Qin — that, too, is false.

sentences.zh: 始公孫季功、董生與夏無且游，具知其事，為余道之如是。

translations.literal: In the beginning, Gongsun Jigong and Master Dong were friends of Xia Wuju, and they knew the full account. They related it to me just as I have set it down.
translations.idiomatic: In earlier times, Gongsun Jigong and Master Dong were friends of Xia Wuju and knew the full account of what happened. They related it to me just as I have set it down here.

sentences.zh: 自曹沫至荊軻五人，此其義或成或不成，然其立意較然，不欺其志，名垂後世，豈妄也哉！

translations.literal: From Cao Mo to Jing Ke, these five men — some succeeded in their righteous cause and some did not, yet their intentions were plain for all to see. They did not betray their convictions, and their names have been handed down through the ages. Is this not fitting?
translations.idiomatic: From Cao Mo to Jing Ke, these five men pursued their cause — some succeeded and some did not. Yet their intentions were plain for all to see. They never betrayed their convictions, and their names have been passed down through the ages. How could this be without reason?

translations.literal: Grand Historian said: World speaks Jing Ke, its praises crown prince Dan fate, 'heaven rained grain, horse grew horns', too excessive. Again speaks Jing Ke injured Qin king, all not so. Beginning Gong Sun Ji Gong, Dong Sheng with Xia Wuqian traveled, fully knew affairs, for me told as this. From Cao Mo to Jing Ke five persons, this their righteousness or completed or not completed, yet their established intent clearly bright, not deceived their will, name hung later generations, how false!
